From His infinite knowledge, Allah has provided humanity with a complete way of life in this world, which is called Islam. The divine revelation affirms: ⟪ar-quote:ان الدین عند اللہ الاسلام⟫

## The World Stands Upon Justice

Corruption appeared upon land and sea through the deeds of human hands, so that people might taste the consequence of their actions and thus return to the right path. This principle, embedded throughout the Qur'ānic narrative, reveals a divine pedagogy: when the conduct of nations becomes corrupted, the chastisement of the Lord falls upon them like rain.

The first revelation Allah granted to humanity was His name. "I am God"—this utterance declares the Divine reality. Yet a name without attributes remains incomplete; caste is known by its qualities. ## Identity

When a person attempts to solve the world's problems through his own intellect and effort alone, confusion multiplies rather than diminishes. Should he chart his own path, he becomes a wanderer upon unseen roads. Should he establish his own priorities, he inherits only desires and failures. ## Pre-Islamic Conditions

When the seventh century began, merciful conceptions of the divine gradually deteriorated into satanic ones. The monotheistic concept of God fragmented into polytheism. Since the Arabs believed that God controlled all benefit and harm, they came to worship whatever seemed to profit or injure them. Scholars of wisdom affirm that the intellect apprehends reality through three pathways:

The first method—⟪ar:حواس خمسۃ⟫ (the five senses)—comprehends the principles of things through the powers of sight, hearing, smell, taste, and tactile sensation. All objects possessing corporeal extension are perceived through ⟪ar:حواس خمسۃ⟫.

To know ⟪ar:مبدع تعالی⟫ is to understand His ⟪ar:توحید⟫ (unity); His monotheism is the appellation for His absolute distinction from creation. The abstraction (⟪ar:تجرید⟫) of the Messenger of Allah from all created beings and their attributes, and the negation of all innovations—this abstraction entails the removal of all ⟪ar:الہیت، أبداع الذوات⟫ (divinity, the origination of essences) from all ⟪ar:مُبدَعات⟫ (originated beings) and all ⟪ar:مخلوقات⟫ (created things), and adherence only to ⟪ar:مبدع تعالی⟫

The Kalima Tawhid comprises two distinct yet inseparable parts: the first is the negation ⟪ar:لا الہ⟫ ("there is no god"), and the second is the affirmation ⟪ar:الا اللہ⟫ ("except Allah"). The first chapter of the Kalima Tawhid is constituted by negation ⟪ar:لا الہ⟫ ("there is no god") and affirmation ⟪ar:الا اللہ⟫ ("except Allah"). The Kalima stands as a testimony to the existence and uniqueness of Allah the Almighty. Within this testimony, the person becomes the witness, while Allah becomes the one to whom testimony is borne—the servant

Testimony corresponds to the enumeration of one and two—negation comes first, affirmation second. These are the numbers of one and two, spiritual and individual. There are two fundamental modes of bringing into being: divinity (al-ilāhiyya) and creation. ## The Divinity and Chieftaincy of the First Intellect

In Arabic, to make a noun definite, one adds the definite article (ḥarf al-maʿrifa (al), ⟪ar:حروف معرفۃ (ال)⟫). For example, "a book" (kitāb) may refer to any book, but "the book" (al-kitāb) indicates a particular one. The three letters of "the divine" (al-ilāh, ⟪ar:الہ⟫)—alif, lām, and hāʾ—signify three jewels: knowledge, power, and life. In
atoms.gloss: The Divinity and Chieftaincy of the First Intellect

atoms.text_en:
## The Beautiful Names of the First Intellect

The glory of the First Intellect differs from all others. He journeyed through the spiritual realm and the station of unity without external guidance, becoming a plateau of knowledge. He began wisdom by contemplating not only monotheism, abstraction, and transcendence of the Originator, Most High, but by performing these acts on behalf of all other be
atoms.gloss: The Beautiful Names of the First Intellect

atoms.text_en:
## Why Was the First Intellect Called the Word?

The true intellect is the essence of the First Intellect because he was the first to utter the Word. He testified to his Creator, as the Qur'ān affirms:

> ⟪quran 3:18⟫
> ⟪ar:‏شَهِدَ ٱللَّهُ أَنَّهُۥ لَآ إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ وَٱلْمَلَٓئِكَةُ وَأُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمِ قَآئِمًۢا بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۚ لَآ إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ ‎⟫
> *Allah bears wi
atoms.gloss: Why Was the First Intellect Called the Word?

atoms.text_en:
## Movement and Stillness

The Originator, Most High, brought forth His command through the word "Be" (kun), which comprises two letters. The letter Kāf (ك) signifies movement, and the letter Nūn (ن) signifies stillness.

| **Kāf** | **Nūn** |
| --- | --- |
| Movement | Stillness |

In the second verse of Sūrat al-Fātiḥa—⟪ar-quote:الحمد للہ رب العالمین⟫ "Praise be to Allah, Lord of the Worlds"—the

## The Word of the First Intellect: Remembrances of Divine Light

Wherever there is emanation (inbiʿāth, ⟪ar:انبعاث⟫), there are correspondingly more forms. The same applies to remembrances (anwār dhikriyya, ⟪ar:أنوار ذکریہ⟫). The word Qur'ān derives from the root meaning "connection," signifying that the Qur'ān is bound to the People of the House (Ahl al-Bayt, ⟪ar:آل⟫). Whatever the People of the
atoms.gloss: The Word of the First Intellect: Remembrances of Divine Light

atoms.text_en:
# Originators



## The Emanators

When the al-ʿAql al-Awwal (First Intellect) attained perfection from the Creator and ten envelopes of joy were placed upon it, two ⟪ar:اشباح صوریہ⟫ proceeded forward together, unaffected by this state of intimacy. Both observed the First Intellect and understood that this condition arose because it had performed tawhīd (divine unification) toward the Almighty Cre

## The Difference in the Perspectives of the Emanators

The First Intellect established the tawhīd of the Almighty through the dual path of negation and affirmation within the utterance of sincere devotion. Because the Shahāda—"*Lā ilāha illā ʾAllāh*" (There is no god but Allah)—contains only these two components, with no third way to approach the Creator's monotheism, the First Intellect made bot
atoms.gloss: The Difference in the Perspectives of the Emanators

atoms.text_en:
## The Difference in the Testimonies of the First Two

The First Intellect moved first toward tawhīd of the Creator of the Almighty and attained secondary perfection. Although the First Emanator attained perfection after the First Intellect, he was first in his own ⟪ar:انبعاث⟫ emanation, and in this respect he also achieved an individual status. For this reason, the First Intellect and the First E
atoms.gloss: The Difference in the Testimonies of the First Two

atoms.text_en:
## The Holy Sanctuary of the First Emanator

The First Intellect established tawhīd without any ⟪ar:ملحم و معلم⟫ guide or teacher, and was established in the station of unity. Witnessing this, the al-Munbaʿithūn moved forward—the First Emanator attaining secondary perfection while the Second remained behind because of his ⟪ar:وهم مساوات⟫ illusion of equivalence. When the First Emanator performed t
atoms.gloss: The Holy Sanctuary of the First Emanator

atoms.text_en:
## The Universal Intellect and the Universal Self

⟪ar:عقل اور نفس، روح (کمال اول)⟫ The Intellect and the Self are names of faculties. Allah the Almighty ⟪ar:ارواح⟫ created the universe from the Spirit, which is why this realm is called ⟪ar:عالم روحانی⟫ the Spiritual World. By harnessing the powers of this Spirit, ⟪ar:عقل⟫ the Intellect is produced—the more perfected form of the Spirit *bi-l-fiʿl*
atoms.gloss: The Universal Intellect and the Universal Self

atoms.text_en:
## The Sign of Definition: Alif and Lam

In Arabic, Alif and Lam are known as the symbols of *Maʿrifa* (definite knowledge) or the article of definition. Therefore, if a noun whose meaning is indefinite—for instance ⟪ar:رجل⟫ (a man)—is to become known, one adds Alif and Lam before it: ⟪ar:الرجل⟫ (the man). Thus in Arabic, every noun preceded by Alif and Lam becomes known and determinate. Alif is t
atoms.gloss: The Sign of Definition: Alif and Lam

atoms.text_en:
## The Inkwell, the Pen, and the Tablet

The Messenger of Allah (upon him be the peace and blessings of Allah) said: "First Allah created the Pen, and then He produced a stream from it—sweeter than honey, whiter than milk, and softer than musk. Then He commanded the Pen: 'Write!' The Pen asked, 'What shall I write?' He said, 'Write down all that shall occur until the Day of Resurrection!' And the
atoms.gloss: The Inkwell, the Pen, and the Tablet

atoms.text_en:
# Sat Minds



## Spiritual Invitation and the Seven Intellects

When both the al-ʿAql al-Awwal (the First Intellect) and the first emanator attained kamāl thānī (secondary perfection), they resolved that they should unite and summon all ⟪ar:اشباح نورانیہ⟫the luminous forms so that these beings, like themselves, might succeed in the tawhīd (monotheistic devotion) of the Creator and attain secondar

## Circle and Its Center

The chiefs and members of the circle were all called ⟪ar:عقول⟫intellects, and among them a hierarchy of highest to lowest was established. Yet the last member of the sixth circle of intellect surpassed the chief of the seventh, though the former held membership while the latter held leadership. The First Intellect, having made no invitation, formed no circle; only the one

## The Circles of the Intellects

When the people of tawhīd advanced toward the Creator of tawhīd, they were joined by their circles, whom they called the Raʾīs (chief). When the first emanator moved toward the tawhīd of the Creator, a number of souls came forward, met his gaze, and became monotheistic in his monotheism. For this state he prays: ⟪ar-quote:النَّظَرُ بِنَظَرِہِ و اَلبَصَرُ بِبَصَرِہ

## The Essence of the Word in Monotheism

The actions (movements) that occurred in the spiritual world correspond to the letters—alif, lām, and hāʾ—in the word "God" ⟪ar:'الہ'⟫. First, the intellect advanced from its own self toward the self without any external summons. This is the state of alif ⟪ar:' ا '⟫. Observing this, and without any daʿwa, another proceeded toward it—this is the state of lā
atoms.gloss: The Essence of the Word in Monotheism

atoms.text_en:
## "God Bears Witness That There Is No God But He"

When the seven intellects were established, the First Intellect manifested as ⟪ar:نور شعشعانی⟫radiant luminescence. Witnessing this ⟪ar:نور شعشعانی⟫radiant light, all the intellects were astonished and astounded, seeing His greatness and exaltation. At that moment, all these intellects acknowledged the First Intellect and conferred upon Him the N
atoms.gloss: "God Bears Witness That There Is No God But He"

atoms.text_en:
# Formation of the world of life



## Their Slip: A Momentary Lapse

Along with Asher's repentance, a group offered their repentance, which ⟪ar:تاَبُو بِتَوبَتِہِ⟫ was called. This was followed by the feast of Asher—⟪ar:تابو بتوبتہ⟫ the last person in the circle—which led to another group called ⟪ar:لب النداہ⟫ the Brotherhood. All three ⟪ar:عاشر، تابو بتوبتہ، لبو النداہ⟫ of them achieved perfecti
atoms.gloss: Formation of the world of life

atoms.text_en:
## The Formation of the World of Life

The group ⟪ar:کلمم⟫ hesitated to accept Asher's invitation and refused acceptance, yet they repented before his order could be executed. Here came a delicate moment for Asher—⟪ar:لب النداہ⟫ the last person in his circle—to determine what would become of the group: whether they would be punished or pardoned. He looked upward with longing and beseeched those ab
atoms.gloss: The Formation of the World of Life

atoms.text_en:
### Adam of the Beginning

As soon as the command ⟪ar:اھبطو⟫ came, one from the ⟪ar:کلمم⟫ tribe, ⟪ar:انحدار⟫ descending in repentance, became monotheistic after profound shame and became what is called ⟪ar:آدم ابدائیۃ⟫—Adam of the Beginning. Beholding this, twelve persons separated themselves, they who had gazed upon him and repented together with his repentance. Then twenty-seven others split awa

## The Summons of the *Kalmam* and Their Ranks

When ⟪ar:عالم حیات⟫ the World of Life was formed, ⟪ar:کلمم⟫ the *Kalmam* were ordered to invite all who ⟪ar:مستودع و مستقر ائمہ⟫ were settled and entrusted as Imams. There existed a crucial difference between their invitations and those of the intellects:

| 1 | Until now, only the last person of the circle had been invited (individual invitation); b
atoms.gloss: The Summons of the *Kalmam* and Their Ranks

atoms.text_en:
## The Classification of the *Kalmam*

### Sight and Vision

The noblest sight is that which is born through struggle and effort in Asher's summons. In the same way that the caste accepted Ashir's invitation, their eyes were fixed upon him. The people of noble sight continue to receive the light of God. Those who attained the highest rank had their sight established through the attention of ⟪ar:'ح

## The Veiled and the Veil

All the Imams and the Pure Ones (al-Asfiyāʾ) are established in the stations ⟪ar:کلمم⟫ of the *Kalmam*. Since they had accepted the summons before they became material, the justice of Allah does not permit them to be sent into this world, which is the abode of Allah's wrath. Yet all of these ⟪ar:کلمم⟫ *Kalmam* make an impression in this material world. This effect is ba

## The Pillar of Light

⟪ar:عمود نور،⟫ In truth, the Pillar of Light ⟪ar:نور کلمہ⟫—the Light of the Word—is another name for ⟪ar:علم جاری و ساری⟫ the Flowing and Pervading Knowledge that has been passed from the Creator to the First Intellect. Whoever came into contact with this light truly succeeded. Then from the First Intellect this light connected with the Second Intellect, and then descended

# Formation of the world of universe and corruption

*Source: KASHKOLE, Binder 1 (Science of Origin and Resurrection), Chapter 80. 20 topics. Language: ur.*



## The Three Intentions of al-ʿĀshir

In shaping the physical world, al-ʿĀshir al-Mudabbir al-Ḥakīm held three principal intentions:

| 1 | Creating Man in a Thousand Ways |
| --- | --- |
| 2 | To imprison the disobedient faction within the
atoms.gloss: Formation of the world of universe and corruption

atoms.text_en:
## The Creation of the Universe in Six Motions

Al-ʿĀshir executed a total of six motions (ḥarakat) in the formation of the physical world, composed of six ⟪ar:مُحَرِّک⟫ (movers). The Qurʾān testifies to this:

> ⟪quran 50:38⟫
> ⟪ar:‏وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍۢ وَمَا مَسَّنَا مِن لُّغُوبٍۢ ‎⟫
> *And indeed We created the heavens and the earth
atoms.gloss: The Creation of the Universe in Six Motions

atoms.text_en:
## The First Motion: Length, Width, and Depth

Al-ʿĀshir resolved to fashion the physical world from the disobedient faction and to confine them within it. Yet in their spiritual state, their imprisonment was impossible; he was therefore obliged to transmute them into corporeality first. The analogy is of a candle burning upon a tablet: one cannot remove the candle without its tablet.[^cite-4] In
atoms.gloss: The First Motion: Length, Width, and Depth

atoms.text_en:
## Primordial Matter and Secondary Matter: Hylē I and Hylē II

When the three motions were completed, all beings took on the nature of ⟪ar:ھیولی⟫ (hylē, primordial matter) and ⟪ar:صورت⟫ (ṣūra, form), and owing to the ⟪ar:ثقل⟫ (weight) of the hylē they began to densify. This is why all things in the present age are encompassed within these three dimensions.

Within this collective mass, those indiv
atoms.gloss: Primordial Matter and Secondary Matter: Hylē I and Hylē II

atoms.text_en:
## The Prayer of the Descending Repentant

When ⟪ar:أنجماد مآء⟫ the freezing of waters occurred and Hylē was commanded to descend, al-ʿĀshir and ⟪ar:اہل کلمم⟫ those of his word (the celestial ministers) gazed upon the repentant faction with a look of longing and mercy, consoling them. The Qurʾān preserves the divine pledge:

> ⟪quran 2:38⟫
> ⟪ar:‏قُلْنَا ٱهْبِطُوا۟ مِنْهَا جَمِيعًۭا ۖ فَإِمَّا يَأ
atoms.gloss: The Prayer of the Descending Repentant

atoms.text_en:
## The Second Motion: The Attraction of the Two Poles (Tajādhub al-Qutbayn)

The terminology of celestial mechanics requires clarification:

| Term | Definition |
| --- | --- |
| Flak (Sphere) | Orbit |
| Kokab (Star) | Planet |
| Stars | Fixed stars |
| Rotation | Spinning in place |
| Durān | Orbiting |

The zodiacal sphere revolves (orbits) only; the seven planets both rotate and revolve; the f
atoms.gloss: The Second Motion: The Attraction of the Two Poles (Tajādhub al-Qutbayn)

atoms.text_en:
## The Governors of the Spheres and the Zodiac

These four forces became magnets of subtlety, drawing subtle essence upward from the hylē. Through their attraction, the most refined beings—the ⟪ar:نادمین و مستغفرین⟫ (Repentant and Seeking Forgiveness)—gathered at the periphery; the ⟪ar:شاک و متحیر⟫ (Vacillating and Bewildered) assembled at the middle; and the ⟪ar:مصر و مستکبرین⟫ (Obdurate and Arro
atoms.gloss: The Governors of the Spheres and the Zodiac

atoms.text_en:
## The Third Motion: The Generation of the Celestial Spheres

Before examining the third motion, we must clarify the doctrine of temperaments (mizāj):

| Temperament (Mizāj) | The four fundamental natures: fire, air, water, and earth—or heat, cold, moisture, and dryness |
| --- | --- |
| Blended (Mumtazij) | The mixture and combination of temperaments |

Al-ʿĀshir then imparted a third motion to t
atoms.gloss: The Third Motion: The Generation of the Celestial Spheres

atoms.text_en:
## The Generation of Time and Space

With the eruption of the cosmic vessel, physicality came into being, and with physicality came time and space. The ⟪ar:کواکب سابتۃ⟫ (fixed stars) and ⟪ar:کواکب سیارۃ⟫ (wandering planets) undergo no corruption or alteration; neither do they diminish nor increase in magnitude. Their motion is purely circular, a form that signifies the perfection of celestial move
atoms.gloss: The Generation of Time and Space

atoms.text_en:
## The Fourth Motion: The Generation of the Stars and the Terrestrial Sphere

Al-ʿĀshir circulated the hylē a fourth time through the sphere of the heavens, an action that caused the heavy elements within the heavens to gather and crystallize.

### The Fixed Stars (Kawākib Thābita) and the Wandering Planets (Kawākib Sāyyāra)

Like the human form, the heavens were gradually differentiated and arran
atoms.gloss: The Fourth Motion: The Generation of the Stars and the Terrestrial Sphere

atoms.text_en:
## The Malefic Stars: Saturn and Mars

Saturn and Mars, though composed of the elements that constitute the Repentant, are designated ⟪ar:نحسین⟫ (the Two Malefic Stars). Their malefic character arises from the fact that some among their order initially accepted the call of Iblīs and embraced rebellion, only to repent subsequently. This circumstance attached misfortune to them. Yet because their re
atoms.gloss: The Malefic Stars: Saturn and Mars

atoms.text_en:
## The Fifth Movement: Creation of the Asdāf and the Mothers

When the Kawākib (celestial bodies) and Saqqara were brought into readiness, a subtle smoke, ⟪ar:دُخَّانِ لَطِیف⟫, which had belonged to the faction of Nadmin (the rebellious ones), remained about Saqqara in accordance with the Divine Will. Since these elements were part of Nadmin, they could neither unite with Saqqara nor join the Umma
atoms.gloss: The Fifth Movement: Creation of the Asdāf and the Mothers

atoms.text_en:
## Creation of the Four Mothers (*Ummahat*)

Countless millions of years elapsed as these heavens and principles formed themselves. The various acts set in motion by Asher occurred at intervals stretching across different epochs. Some of these intervals were brief; others immeasurably long. After the Originator granted the heavens a total of six movements, the earth acquired the capacity to move o

## The Jauhar, the Two Knots, the Head, and the Tail

Beneath the moon are two wandering stars, ⟪ar:جوزہر⟫, which possess neither shell nor dense body; they are named after their positions: ⟪ar:'راس'⟫ (the Head) and ⟪ar:'زنب'⟫ (the Tail). These are both malefic stars in which the Tail exhibits wickedness more intense than the Head. Both are called ⟪ar:عقدتین⟫ (the Two Knots).[^cite-12] These two a
atoms.gloss: The Jauhar, the Two Knots, the Head, and the Tail

atoms.text_en:
## The Gates of Paradise and Hell

Paradise and Hell are one and the same in essence, yet they are reached through different portals—a difference of form. The gates form a series of levels surrounding the centers. The gates ascending toward Paradise grow ever more subtle and refined; the gates descending toward Hell grow ever more dense and heavy. Paradise possesses eight gates; Hell possesses sev
atoms.gloss: The Gates of Paradise and Hell

atoms.text_en:
## The Greater Sensibility and the Greater Generative Power

The cosmos is divided into two principal realms: ⟪ar:عالم جرمانی⟫ (the world of dense bodies—from ⟪ar:فلک محیط⟫ to ⟪ar:تحت فلک قمر⟫) and ⟪ar:عالم جسمانی⟫ (the world of subtle bodies—from ⟪ar:تحت فلک قمر⟫ to ⟪ar:سقرا⟫), called ⟪ar:' جسم مطلق'⟫ (the Absolute Body), where the physical universe begins. ⟪ar:فلک محیط⟫ is also termed ⟪ar:ھیولیٰ
atoms.gloss: The Greater Sensibility and the Greater Generative Power

atoms.text_en:
## The Mingled Temperament

When these four spheres were arranged, their intermingling created one subtle smoke, ⟪ar:دُخان لطیف⟫. ⟪ar:عالم حیات⟫ The Ten Intellects turned their gaze toward their respective heavens. That is, in the World of Life, the intellect standing in the position of al-ʿAql al-Awwal (the First Intellect) focused upon ⟪ar:فلک محیط⟫ (the Encompassing Heaven), the second intellec

## The Sixth Movement: The Governance of the Heavens

When all the hyle, ⟪ar:ھیولی⟫, had been expended in creating the physical world, and the heavens were divided into their proper ranks—the heavens themselves, the Mothers, and Saqqara—Asher gave the heavens their sixth and final movement, which inaugurated the schemes of celestial governance, ⟪ar:تدبیرات الافلاک⟫. Each heaven devised and perfect
atoms.gloss: The Sixth Movement: The Governance of the Heavens

atoms.text_en:
## The Durations: Dūr, Kūr, Tūr, and Mūr

### The Dūr (Cycle)

These seven heavens were entrusted with the task of influencing the world through their own governance schemes and bringing down the clear essences, ⟪ar:قامت الفیۃ⟫, into Saqqara. Then, through the Imam's infidelity and godhood, they grant them death and raise them again—transforming them from the ⟪ar:ھیکل (کثافت)⟫ (temple of density)
atoms.gloss: The Durations: Dūr, Kūr, Tūr, and Mūr

atoms.text_en:
## The Conditions of the Cycles of Unveiling and Concealment

Just as an ordinary day comprises twenty-four hours and is divided into three periods—morning, afternoon, and night—so too does the Divine Day possess a religious duration of fifty-seven thousand years, ⟪ar:دور کشف، فترت اور ستر⟫. The daytime extends for fifty thousand years, with the first portion being forty-seven thousand years, whic
atoms.gloss: The Conditions of the Cycles of Unveiling and Concealment

atoms.text_en: # Births

## The Heavens' Creations

The principal purpose governing all the celestial spheres was to generate minerals, plants, animals, and human seed ⟪ar:(سلالۃ)⟫. In their appointed succession, each sphere exerted its influence upon this terrestrial realm according to its nature, extracting and constituting diverse elements, and bringing them forth upon the earth. These four categories of being are cons

## The Birth of Humanity from the Pits

When the Moon came to aid Saturn, the process of human generation commenced. This is because the Moon is the seventh heaven, holding a particular station in the ⟪ar:'خلق آخر'⟫ ultimate creation of humanity. At the beginning of each age, the same pits are born from ⟪ar:جثہ ابدائیۃ⟫ primordial bodies.

Over six thousand years, all lawful minerals, plants, and
atoms.gloss: The Birth of Humanity from the Pits

atoms.text_en:
## Two Types of Creation

The Divine has created two types of creation in this world. One emerges as vegetation from the earth, occurring at the beginning of each age; the other occurs through birth ⟪ar:'رحم مادر '⟫ from the maternal womb.

⟪ar:اشباح صوریۃ⟫ When these luminous forms appeared in dense state within the three gates, ⟪ar:تدبیر الٰہی⟫ divine governance required them to be scattered and

## The Heavens' Governance in the Formation of the Foetus

### Saturn: (First Month)

In the first month, the human sperm receives the influence of Saturn, for it is first among the heavens. The nature of this sphere is to coagulate. In the generation from pits, Saturn solidified the pit's boiling water and froze it, allowing the water to cease its boiling and emerge from the pit. Similarly, in th
atoms.gloss: The Heavens' Governance in the Formation of the Foetus

atoms.text_en:
## The Stages of Foetal Formation

The Qur'an describes the seven stages of foetal birth:

> ⟪quran 23:12-14⟫
> ⟪ar:‏وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَنَ مِن سُلَلَةٍۢ مِّن طِينٍۢ ‎ ‏ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَهُ نُطْفَةًۭ فِى قَرَارٍۢ مَّكِينٍۢ ‎ ‏ثُمَّ خَلَقْنَا ٱلنُّطْفَةَ عَلَقَةًۭ فَخَلَقْنَا ٱلْعَلَقَةَ مُضْغَةًۭ فَخَلَقْنَا ٱلْمُضْغَةَ عِظَمًۭا فَكَسَوْنَا ٱلْعِظَمَ لَحْمًۭا ثُمَّ أَنشَأْنَهُ خَلْقًا آخَرَ ۚ ف

## The Senses and the Powers of the Brain

Those parts of the body that are firm and solid are called ⟪ar:'اعضا '⟫ organs; those that are in the form of vaporous essences—very subtle, pure, and transparent—arising from these four qualities and pervading the body are ⟪ar:'مزاجات '⟫ temperaments; and the subtle substance itself is ⟪ar:'روح حیوانی '⟫ the animal soul.

| Solid | Intermediate | Subtle
atoms.gloss: The Senses and the Powers of the Brain

atoms.text_en:
## Humanity as the Quintessence of the Universe

⟪ar:صاحب رسائل اخوان الصفا⟫ The author of the Epistles of the Brethren of Purity explains that ⟪ar:'عالم'⟫ the world refers to the seven heavens, the earth, and all that lies between them. They also call it ⟪ar:'انسان الکبیر '⟫ the Macrocosm or Great Human. This is because, like humanity, the world itself possesses a body with distinct organs. This
atoms.gloss: Humanity as the Quintessence of the Universe

atoms.text_en: # People and their Types

## What Are Souls and How Do They Emerge?

There is a life force that exists in a hidden state throughout the world. When the darkness of the spiritual realm descended, it fell into the form of substance and shape. Therefore, whatever exists in this universe today manifests in the form of *hyle* (ḥūlī, matter) and form; whatever bears the character of *namiyyah* (نامیۃ, the vegetative principle) r
atoms.gloss: What Are Souls and How Do They Emerge?

atoms.text_en:
## Types of Souls

The self is singular in essence, yet its forces emerge at different times and stations. The rise of these forces has received distinct names. When the fetus in the mother's womb possesses only the power to grow, it is named ⟪ar:نفس نامیۃ⟫ the vegetative soul. When the five senses activate outside the womb, it receives the name ⟪ar:نفس حسیۃ⟫ the sensible soul. When the powers of

## The Vegetative Soul (*Nafs al-Namiyyah*)

The Commander of the Faithful states that ⟪ar:نفس نامیۃ⟫ it is the designation of a physical force whose origin lies in four temperaments (*ṭabʿīyyāt*): heat, constipation, cold, and moisture—which unite when sperm forms in the womb. Within the human body, the ⟪ar:نفس نامیہ⟫ vegetative soul has its seat in the liver. ⟪ar:نفس نامیۃ⟫ Its sustenance comes

## The Sensible Soul (*Nafs al-Ḥassiyya*)

The Commander of the Faithful states that ⟪ar:نفس حسیۃ⟫ the sensible soul is so called because its origin lies in ⟪ar:'فلکی قوت'⟫ celestial force, and its center is the ⟪ar:'افلاک'⟫ spheres, with the ⟪ar:'شمس'⟫ sun as its governing seat. It is grounded in ⟪ar:ولادت جسمانیۃ⟫ corporeal generation and has its roots ⟪ar:'قلب'⟫ in the human heart. Its function

## The Rational Soul (*Nafs al-Nāṭiqa*)

The newborn learns gradually from ⟪ar:نطق مجازی⟫ his parents through metaphorical speech until he attains adulthood. This ⟪ar:نطق مجازی⟫ capacity for speech distinguishes him from animals, for although animals possess ⟪ar:'بولی'⟫ sound, they lack ⟪ar:نطق⟫ rational utterance. Because of this faculty, humans are able to utter meaningful words. When he learns

## The Universal Soul (*Nafs al-Kulliyya*)

The Commander of the Faithful states that ⟪ar:نفس کلیۃ⟫ the universal soul is ⟪ar:قوت الہیۃ⟫ a divine force. His essence is vast, and He is the All-Knower of power and deed. It is the origin ⟪ar:'عالم انوار'⟫ from which one seeks help and to whom one calls. And he is ⟪ar:کلمۃ اللہ⟫ the word of Allāh, ⟪ar:شجرۃ طوبۃ⟫ the tree of Tūbā, ⟪ar:جنت الماوا⟫ close

## The Sensible Soul Is the Fundamental Soul

Allāh declares in many verses that His signs encompass the heavens and the earth:

Since the fallen beings are divided into categories based on their own remorse—⟪ar:نادمین⟫ the penitent, ⟪ar:شاک و متحیرین⟫ the doubters and the perplexed, and ⟪ar:مصر و مستکبرین⟫ the obstinate and arrogant—these three natures are found in every species and gender. ## How Forbidden Deeds Receive Their Recompense

When a person commits that which is forbidden—transgressing what has been enjoined by no authority—his own ⟪ar:جوہر نفس⟫ essence of soul darkens swiftly. His wicked deeds obstruct him from the path of guidance; they soil his soul and lead him astray, ensnaring him in sin's snare. These misdeeds then incite enmity toward the limits (ḥudūd) of truth a
atoms.gloss: How Forbidden Deeds Receive Their Recompense

atoms.text_en:
## The Death and Return of the Righteous

Those who, though they take not the pledge of the Imam, follow the outward commandments of Islam—who do not oppress, who remain chaste, pious, benevolent, truthful, and faithful; who do not recognize the guardianship of the Prophet and his successor, yet harbor no rancor toward the Imams—such are called the ⟪ar:'صلحاء'⟫ righteous. When they depart this lif
atoms.gloss: The Death and Return of the Righteous

atoms.text_en:
## The Death and Return of the Disbeliever

Save for ⟪ar:مقاصرین⟫ the defaulting believers and ⟪ar:صلحا⟫ the righteous, all other people merit punishment and endure grievous torments at death. After death, they undergo the most severe afflictions. A slight difference exists between the death of a Muslim and a disbeliever—provided the Muslim harbors no rancor against ʿAlī and his descendants.

**A
atoms.gloss: The Death and Return of the Disbeliever

atoms.text_en:
## The Wandering of Evil Forms

After forty days, when this evil form separates from the body, it shapes itself as it wishes and acts as it desires. Having departed the body's confinement, it has reached the extremity of its wickedness. While embodied, its powers were limited—it was ⟪ar:شیطان بالقوۃ⟫ a satan in potentiality; now separated, restraint vanishes and it becomes ⟪ar:شیطان بالفعل⟫ a sata

## Evil Forms as the Opposites of the Sacred Stations

There is a gathering of wicked concepts—the ⟪ar:عقدتین⟫ two knots. They are granted respite so that the person with whom they are to be reckoned may appear in this world. When both are present and the hour of retribution comes, then the ⟪ar:صورات خبیثہ⟫ evil forms complete their ⟪ar:قصاصات⟫ retribution. By the will of the wise one, these verse
atoms.gloss: Evil Forms as the Opposites of the Sacred Stations

atoms.text_en:
## Munkar and Nakīr (The Evil Form)

⟪ar:آنحضرت صلعم⟫ According to the Messenger of Allah, it is well known that at the moment of death, two angels appear—⟪ar:'مبشروبشیر'⟫ bearers of glad tidings and bearers of tidings. The reality is that the Speaker and Guardian of every age is, for those who love him, a ⟪ar:مبشر و بشیر⟫ herald of good news, whilst for those who hate him he is a ⟪ar:منکر و نکیر⟫

## The Lesser Torments

After death, the believer's body becomes the self—his development—while the disbeliever's soul disperses into the body so that torment upon the body reaches his feeling. Soul and body unite and merge with the soil, then rise like vapor and fall like rain. This rain produces lightning and thunder—deadly strikes—and floods that drown nations and destroy fields, producing a co

## Employed Forms

Their bodies suffer punishment; their evil form inflicts torment upon this body. Upon completing punishment, they are invited to the Imam. If they refuse, the ⟪ar:جن مزموم⟫ condemned jinn remains, proceeding to the next punishment. If ⟪ar:اسلام⟫ he submits and repents, he becomes a ⟪ar:'جن محمود '⟫ commendable jinn. He then ascends through the moon to the sun, where services are

## The Middle Torments

These are the four corners of the earth where climate is not temperate, where extremes of weather prevail. Great and terrible calamities abound. Only one already punished with this torment arrives at ⟪ar:جہنم کبریٰ⟫ the Great Hell—these are the stations of the Nāṭiq and the Imams.

The north is dark, painful, terrifying—th

After the torments of the sides, they are led to the Path ⟪ar:ربع مسکون⟫ (the inhabited quarter of the earth) where they ⟪ar:مزاج و ممتزج⟫ appear in the Garden of the ʿAzāʾis. They are then presented before the Qāʾim, and ⟪ar:قامت الفیہ⟫ thereafter led to the Heaven of the Living. ⟪ar:عذاب اکبر⟫

Among those appearing are those who have turned from torment
atoms.gloss: Return from the Torments of the Sides

atoms.text_en:
## The Great Punishment

All the Hudoods (the Divine Limits), Prophets, and Imams will gather in the assembly ⟪ar:قائم علی ذکرہ السلام⟫, where they shall inform all creation of the deeds of their communities. Thereafter, the Qaim will put them all to death. A fire will descend upon them ⟪ar:عقدتین⟫, consuming their foul and lifeless bodies; as vapor, they will rise into it and be absorbed. When th

Three categories of torment are distinguished:

atoms.gloss: The Inhabitants of Lesser and Greater Punishment

atoms.text_en:
## The Description of Saqqara

Our earth is ⟪ar:بیضوی⟫ oval and ⟪ar:مسطح⟫ flat, and on its far side lies ⟪ar:سقرا⟫, which is ⟪ar:سجین⟫ and ⟪ar:جہنم کبریٰ⟫—the Great Hell—⟪ar:مستوی⟫ on its level. The center of the earth's surface aligns with the ⟪ar:کعبۃ اللہ⟫; on the earth's opposite pole stands Saqqara in the form of a hollow, its lowest region ⟪ar:'اہل طاغوت '⟫ the abode of the rebellious. The e

## Emergence from Saqqara

When the period of Saqqara's punishment concludes, they emerge from Saqqara's upper reaches through four apertures, exhaling as vapor into the earth's exterior. One of these four passages lies in a valley ⟪ar:'حضر موت '⟫—Hadramaut—containing a well named ⟪ar:'برہوت '⟫ or ⟪ar:'شفاہُ'⟫. The second sits in the ⟪ar:خراسان (ایران)⟫ Khurasan (Iran) region, called ⟪ar:'موکا '⟫

## Do the Punished Know Their Past Human State?

They are aware. When passing through animal forms and changing their conditions, they are made conscious of the sin for which they suffer. Upon reaching the plant and mineral realms, this knowledge is withdrawn; when cast into Saqqara, they are informed of their offense and those who led them astray. They perpetually strike and slay one another; the
atoms.gloss: Do the Punished Know Their Past Human State?

⟪ar:صاحب رسائل اخوان الصفاء⟫ The author of the Epistles of the Brethren of Purity and ⟪ar:مولانا امام احمد⟫ Mawlānā Imām Ahmad affirm: "⟪ar:صورات خبیثہ⟫ The foul forms—⟪ar:ملائکۃ الغضبان⟫ the Angels of Wrath—drive them forth at the command of ⟪ar:عالم قدس⟫ the Holy World." ⟪ar:عاشر علیہ السلام، عالم طبعیت⟫ The Tenth One upon him be peace, the Worl

## Every Manifest Form Conceals an Inner Reality

The word "Zahir" (ظَہَرَ⟫) derives from the Arabic root denoting the back or exterior, while "Batin" (⟪ar:بَطَنْ⟫) signifies the belly or interior. The vital organs (⟪ar:اعضاء رئیسۃ⟫) of the human body reside not upon the back but within the belly; what is truly real and substantial (⟪ar:بَطَنْ⟫) lies concealed, while the outer form (⟪ar:ظَہَرَ⟫) s
atoms.gloss: Every Manifest Form Conceals an Inner Reality

atoms.text_en:
## The Significance of Parable and Reality

Whosoever follows the Sharīʿa by understanding both the parable and its reality will be counted among the foremost (⟪ar:'سابقین'⟫)—the elect who grasped the truth from the very beginning. Those among the people of truth who, having not discerned the parable from its reality, merely stand by the outer law, shall be among the followers and those who come l
atoms.gloss: The Significance of Parable and Reality

atoms.text_en:
## The Relationship Between Parable and Reality

There exists always a special correspondence between parable and reality: the same qualities present in the reality must also inhere in the parable. This correspondence between parable and reality is termed taʾwīl (⟪ar:'تاویل'⟫), or esoteric exegesis. For this reason, all the speakers of divine law (nuṭaqāʾ) set forth a parable for their Sharīʿa, so
atoms.gloss: The Relationship Between Parable and Reality

Allāh declares in the Qur'ān:

> ⟪ar:‏لِلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْآخِرَةِ مَثَلُ ٱلسَّوْءِ ۖ وَلِلَّهِ ٱلْمَثَلُ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ ‎⟫
> *For those who believe not in the Hereafter is an evil description, and for Allah is the highest description. And He is the All-Mighty, the All-Wise.*

Allāh (⟪ar:'اللہ'⟫) is a self (dhāt) that i

## The Outer and Inner Meanings in the Qur'ān

Allāh the Most High mentions the outward and inward repeatedly in the Qur'ān, declaring: "Allāh has bestowed upon you His blessings both manifest and hidden."

> ⟪quran 31:20⟫
> ⟪ar:‏أَلَمْ تَرَوْا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَخَّرَ لَكُم مَّا فِى ٱلسَّمَوَتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَأَسْبَغَ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعَمَهُۥ ظَهِرَةًۭ وَبَاطِنَةًۭ ۗ وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يُجَدِلُ

atoms.text_en:
## Auspicious and Inauspicious Days

Among the 354 days of the lunar year, certain days and nights hold special significance for the believers; these are exalted occasions. These outer days correspond to the rotation of the celestial spheres and are measured by sunrise and sunset. Yet these manifest days possess no innate knowledge of themselves; they have no awareness of their own comings and goi

## The Pairing of the Months of the Year

Just as all things in the universe are created in pairs, so too Allāh created the months of the year in pairs. Sayyidnā Sulaymān (⟪ar:سیدنا سلیمان صاحب⟫) teaches that Muḥarram al-Ḥarām (⟪ar:محرم الحرام⟫) and Shawwāl (⟪ar:شوال⟫) share the same day of the week on their first of the month, for they are spouses to one another. Muḥarram (⟪ar:محرم⟫) has thirty d
atoms.gloss: The Pairing of the Months of the Year

atoms.text_en:
## The Higher and Lower Perspectives in the Year

Of the 354 days in a lunar year, one hundred are particularly blessed. Among these, thirty-six are supremely auspicious, divided into two categories of perspective: the Higher Perspective (Naẓar Ashraf) belonging to the Imams, and the Lower Perspective (Naẓar Adūn) belonging to their representatives. Each category contains eighteen days.

| Higher

Amīr al-Muʾminīn ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib (⟪ar:أمیر المؤمنین علی ابن ابی طالب علیہ السلام⟫) declared: "Acquire understanding of the religion first, then engage in commerce." ## The Speakers of Divine Law and the Difference in Their Sharīʿas

Sayyidnā Idrīs ibn Ḥasan (⟪ar:سیدنا ادریس بن حسن اعلی اللہ قدسہ⟫), expounding this philosophy, states: "From Adam (⟪ar:رسول اللہ (ﷺ)⟫) there have been six speakers of the law (nuṭaqāʾ). The common understanding differs fundamentally from the truth. The masses believe that each prophet abolished the law preceding him and promulgate
atoms.gloss: The Speakers of Divine Law and the Difference in Their Sharīʿas

## The Speaker and the Divine Law: Their Codification

One of the pillars of faith, and the most robust of its foundations, is adherence to the Sharīʿa (⟪ar:'شریعت'⟫). Through the divine law (nāmūs) (⟪ar:'ناموس'⟫), the speaker commands and forbids. All such ordinances rest upon the divine law (⟪ar:أوامر و نواحی⟫). The nāmūs is the straight path whose destination is Paradise, for those who follow i
atoms.gloss: The Speaker and the Divine Law: Their Codification

atoms.text_en:
## Esoteric Interpretation and the Circles of Reality

The Messenger of Allāh (⟪ar:رسول اللہ (ﷺ)⟫) declared: "Allāh has founded His religion upon the parable of His creation, so that through the religion you may establish proof of the world, and through the world proof of the religion." That is, Allāh created the world as a parable (⟪ar:'مثال'⟫) and religion as its reality (⟪ar:'ممثول'⟫). The name
atoms.gloss: Esoteric Interpretation and the Circles of Reality

atoms.text_en: # Wisdom and Knowledge

## The Station of Intellect and Knowledge in the World

The learned agree that the universe rests upon a fundamental principle: existence precedes non-existence. From this foundation, two types of being emerge: the animate (possessing the power to grow) and the inanimate (unchanging), with animation taking precedence over stasis. Among animate beings, two categories arise: the sentient and the non
atoms.gloss: The Station of Intellect and Knowledge in the World

atoms.text_en:
## The True Purpose of Intellect

The intellect is the greatest gift Allah has bestowed upon humanity. Through the wisdom of intellect, man has brought down the birds of the air and drawn forth fish from the river's depths, subjugating beasts and birds to his dominion. He benefits from their flesh and hides, and despite the vastness of the sky, he has bound it within the prison of his thought. Had

## The Union of Knowledge with Action

Man is constituted of two fundamental principles: ⟪ar:روح⟫ spirit and ⟪ar:جسم⟫ body. Thus he is not a simple (⟪ar:مفرد⟫) being but a compound (⟪ar:مرکب⟫) one. More subtly, the human composite consists of ⟪ar:'روح لطیف'⟫ the refined spirit and ⟪ar:'جسم کثیف'⟫ the dense body. Their union establishes human existence. All creation in this world springs from this
atoms.gloss: The Union of Knowledge with Action

atoms.text_en:
## The Difference in the Progression of Knowledge and Action

Just as the body cannot survive without life, neither can the soul endure without the body. Between them exists such profound interdependence that neither can exist without the other. ⟪ar:سیدنا سلیمان بن حسن اعلی اللہ قدسہ⟫ Our Master Sulaymān ibn al-Ḥasan, may Allah sanctify his secret, taught that a believer requires two wings to asce
atoms.gloss: The Difference in the Progression of Knowledge and Action

atoms.text_en:

When the hadith of the Messenger of Allah—⟪ar-quote:طلب العلم فریضۃ علی کل مسلم و مسلمۃ⟫ "Seeking knowledge is obligatory upon every Muslim man and woman"—was presented to Mawlānā ʿAlī (peace be upon him), he clarified: ⟪ar-quote:العلم التقوی و الیقین⟫ "That knowledge is piety and certitude," referring to the ⟪ar:کلمہ توحید لا الہ الا اللہ⟫ testimony of unity: "Th

## Truth and Conjecture

The supplication declares: ⟪ar-quote:اَلحَقُّ یَعلُو وَلاَ یُعلاَ⟫ "Truth is exalted, nothing exalts it." When the people of truth hear the words Allah has revealed, their eyes overflow with tears—they declare, "Our Lord, we believe! Write us among the witnesses!" Allah says:

> ⟪quran 5:83-86⟫
> ⟪ar:‏وَإِذَا سَمِعُوا۟ مَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَى ٱلرَّسُولِ تَرَىٰٓ أَعْيُنَهُمْ تَفِ

## Humanity and Its Spiritual Advancement

Allah declares:

> ⟪quran 23:14⟫
> ⟪ar:‏ثُمَّ خَلَقْنَا ٱلنُّطْفَةَ عَلَقَةًۭ فَخَلَقْنَا ٱلْعَلَقَةَ مُضْغَةًۭ فَخَلَقْنَا ٱلْمُضْغَةَ عِظَمًۭا فَكَسَوْنَا ٱلْعِظَمَ لَحْمًۭا ثُمَّ أَنشَأْنَهُ خَلْقًا آخَرَ ۚ فَتَبَارَكَ ٱللَّهُ أَحْسَنُ ٱلْخَلِقِينَ ‎⟫

# Qadaf and Qisas



## The Matter of Retribution

The matter of retribution (qisas, قصاص) encompasses all humanity—disbelievers, believers, prophets, and imams alike. The common understanding holds that life persists so long as it exists in the material sense; yet the Qur'an subverts this notion, testifying instead that life itself depends upon the continuance of retribution. Once qisas ceases, l

In explaining the grandeur of the Almighty, the Seal of the Prophets and Messengers (muḥammad ʿalayhi al-salām, محمد علیہ السلام) spoke: ⟪ar-quote:لو لاک لما خلقت الافلاک⟫ "Had it not been for you, I would not have created the heavens."

The mystics and spiritual scholars elucidate this profound utterance as follows: t

## The Matter of Divine Decree and Divine Determination

⟪ar:' قضا '⟫The term *qaḍā* signifies judgment, whilst ⟪ar:' قدر '⟫ *qadar* denotes the power and determination that one possesses. In the human condition, cycles (kurrāt, كرّات) occur such that after every thousand years the world of a person is overturned. The good and evil deeds (ḥasanāt and sayyiʾāt, حسنات and السیئات) of one's previous
atoms.gloss: The Matter of Divine Decree and Divine Determination

atoms.text_en:
## Prophet Dāwūd and the Judgment of the Hereafter

There exist two categories of judgment: one is ⟪ar:'قضاء الدنیا'⟫ the judgment of this world (*qaḍāʾ al-dunyā*), and the other is ⟪ar:'قضاء الآخرۃ'⟫ the judgment of the hereafter (*qaḍāʾ al-ākhirah*). ⟪ar:قضاء الدنیا⟫ *Qaḍāʾ al-dunyā* is rendered upon the basis of the summons (al-daʿwa, الدعوة) and evidence (bayyināt, بَیِّناَت); ⟪ar:قضاء الآخرۃ⟫
atoms.gloss: Prophet Dāwūd and the Judgment of the Hereafter

atoms.text_en:
## Retribution and the Imams' Enforcement of Divine Boundaries

### The Nature of Retribution

There is nothing improper in the matter of retribution within Allah's justice, whether among the believers, the masses, the saints of Allah, the young, or the old. Were forgiveness permissible with anyone, it would surely be with the saints of Allah; yet even they do not stand beyond the reach of qisas.

A beneficiary must first grasp a fundamental truth: without the kindness and favour of his mentor, he will never advance through the ranks of knowledge. He should acknowledge the vastness of his mentor's useful wisdom and his encompassing faith, and confess that the mentor's usefulness surpasses his own in both knowledge and

## The Reflection and Questions of a Beneficiary

Once a beneficiary has secured trust in his mentor regarding ⟪ar:'فکر'⟫ (thought) and ⟪ar:'ذات'⟫ (essence), he should then investigate and pose questions concerning the nuances and subtle details of that truth, seeking rational arguments and proofs from his mentor. Every creature requires sustenance to endure; just so, ⟪ar:نفس حسیہ⟫ (the sensible soul) requires ⟪ar:'علم'⟫ (knowledge) as its nourishment. Just as the body grows weak without outward sustenance, so too does the ⟪ar:نفس حسیۃ⟫ (sensible soul) weaken without knowledge; its ⟪ar:'ملکوتی'⟫ (celestial, angelic) faculties diminish, while its ⟪ar

A sincere beneficiary might employ his time in worldly affairs, rest, or the satisfaction of bodily appetites. Yet instead, in order to please his Lord and to perfect his soul, he binds himself in the bonds of the Hereafter and rises up in the worship of knowledge. This action increases the pleasure of Allah and earns His generous reward.

The thirst for knowledge increases the beneficiary's demand for knowledge. When that thirst reaches its zenith, no force in the world, nor any preoccupation, can prevent him from acquiring knowledge. When a beneficiary poses a good question, sometimes his intellect takes pride in its own acuity, and he feels he has asked the finest question. It is incumbent upon a person to give thanks to Allah with every breath. Indeed! A thousand thanks, and more. Only those who obey His commands remember Allah; whoever forgets Him is disobedient. The sign of guidance is obedience; the sign of error is disobedience. The root of both is "remembrance of Allah." Satan gains dominion over Allah's servants only when they have abandoned His remembrance, grown disdainful when faced with His prohibition, felt complacent in their disobedience, and forgotten that Allah witnesses their secret acts. The whisper (*waswāsa*) is that which enters the heart from without, by the tacit permission of reason, and is sustained by man's own nature. On

Faith conducts a person to every noble station and glorious place. Allah (Exalted is He!) said to His Prophet and loved one, praising him, in order to manifest His blessing upon him,

⟪ar:وَإِنَّكَ لَعَلَىٰ خُلُقٍ عَظِيمٍۢ⟫

Assuredly, thou art of a tremendous character.

atoms.text_en:
## Defining Good and Bad Character

People have discoursed upon the true nature of good character and what it constitutes, but they have in fact only touched the fruit of the tree, and not its roots (reality). In fact, they have not even grasped the entirety of its fruit! There are many quotes on the subject of good character, but most do not explain the reality of what a truly good character is,

## Can Character Be Changed? — Al-Ghazālī's Argument

A man who is dominated by sloth will consider any spiritual struggle, discipline, purification of soul, and refinement of character unpleasant. This is because the deficiencies in his nature and soul will not permit him to undertake such a journey. Such a man therefore will claim that the traits of a man’s character cannot conceivably be altere
atoms.gloss: Can Character Be Changed? — Al-Ghazālī's Argument

atoms.text_en:
## How Good Character Can Be Acquired

We have established that good character proceeds from an equilibrium between the "Rational", "Irascible" and "Appetitive" faculties by their submission to the intellect and the Law (Shareeat). This equilibrium may come about in two ways. First of these, is through Divine grace [Fazl], and completeness of innate disposition [kamaal al-fitra], whereby a man is
atoms.gloss: How Good Character Can Be Acquired

atoms.text_en:
## Methods for Refining Character

You have now learnt that an equilibrium in the traits of character makes the soul healthy, while any deviation from this equilibrium creates a sickness and a disorder within it. This is similar to the fact that as long as the four humors of the body are in balance, it remains healthy, and any imbalance in them makes the body sick. Let us therefore take the human

## Symptoms of the Diseases of the Heart and Their Restoration

Know that each member of your body has been created for a particular function. The member is considered ill if it can no longer perform the act for which it was created. The hand is considered ill when it can no longer strike, and the eye, when it can no longer see. Such is the case with the heart, when it can no longer acquire knowle
atoms.gloss: Symptoms of the Diseases of the Heart and Their Restoration

## How to Identify the Faults of One's Own Soul

Know that when Allah blesses his bondsmen, He grants them insight into the faults of their soul. The faults of an insightful man are never hidden from him, and whoever identifies his faults, is in a position to treat them. Most people, however, are ignorant of the faults that lie in their soul. They notice the tiniest mote in their brother’s eye but
atoms.gloss: How to Identify the Faults of One's Own Soul

If you contemplate on what has been said, your understanding will be brightened, and the diseases and remedies of your hearts will be unveiled to you through the light of knowledge and certitude. The man who is unable to act perfectly should at least make an effort towards perfection. There are four classes of people in this regard who strive:

## Examples of Good Character (from the Prophets and Saints)

Allah has mentioned the traits of believers and hypocrites in his book, which are in turn fruits of good or bad character. He says, "The faithful have triumphed! atoms.gloss: Examples of Good Character (from the Prophets and Saints)

atoms.gloss: Struggling on the Path of Discipline (Mujāhadat al-Nafs)

atoms.text_en: # Interpretation of Surah Al-Fatiha

## The Virtue of Surah Al-Fatiha

⟪ar:سورۃ الفاتحۃ⟫The opening chapter of the Qur'an possesses innumerable spiritual blessings. It has been transmitted that this Surah commands such eminence that it would occasion no surprise if it were recited over the deceased, and through its blessed power the departed soul were to rise again. This mighty chapter is designated ⟪ar:'أم الکتاب'⟫—the Mother of the

## The Ten Names of Surah Al-Fatiha

Surah Al-Fatiha bears ten designations, each corresponding to the ten ranks through which consciousness ascends toward ultimate unity and singular essence (the final stations of primordial self-sufficiency and absolute oneness). The ten ranks represent stations of ʿAyn Awla—the first perspective—wherein the seeker progresses to the ultimate plane of undifferent
atoms.gloss: The Ten Names of Surah Al-Fatiha

atoms.text_en:
## The Jewels (Jawahir) of Surah Al-Fatiha

Though the apparent verses and words of Surah Al-Fatiha may be counted, the luminous jewels emanating from it are infinite and their virtues incalculable. Its outward essence comprises twenty-one letters. Of these twenty-one, fourteen are noorani (radiant jewels). Within these fourteen, seven are *Jawahir Nooraniyyah Khafifa* (subtle radiant jewels) and

## The Esoteric Exegesis (Taʾwīl) of Surah Al-Fatiha

### Bismillah ar-Rahman ar-Rahim

This verse refers to the realm of creation, wherein the Creator brought forth all existents from the beginning of time.

### Al-Hamdu Lillahi Rabbi al-ʿAlamin (Praise Be to Allah, Lord of the Worlds)

⟪ar:'الحمد'⟫Praise belongs to Him—to the First Intellect (al-ʿAql al-Awwal)—by whose praise all the worlds (the
atoms.gloss: The Esoteric Exegesis (Taʾwīl) of Surah Al-Fatiha

atoms.text_en:
## The Seven Opposites of the Accents (Lahja) of Surah Al-Fatiha

The oppressive accents refer to the inscriptions of the alphabet—the pronunciations of the lower veils (⟪ar:'حجب دانیۃ'⟫). The personalities of such people are oppressed, and their souls are sinners devoid of the light of divine support. Their condition resembles a dark house with no lamp kindled within. These are they from whom div
atoms.gloss: The Seven Opposites of the Accents (Lahja) of Surah Al-Fatiha

atoms.text_en:
## Adam and His Opposite

Adam emerged from clay—the oppressive element that carries humankind toward death. Yet Allah breathed into this dead soil His spirit, reviving it, teaching it the truths of all things, and then proclaimed:

Through divine teaching, a succession of radiant lights and blessings descended to Adam. These lights were granted through the letters of the radiant category—the inte

## Surah Al-Fatiha Is the Cause of Intercession

Surah Al-Fatiha became a cure for all spiritual ailments because the oppressive letters—which are defects—have been excluded from it. Having grown weary of multiplication and its attachments, this Surah Al-Fatiha shall elevate us from this world to the celestial realm. A feast involving multiplication shall never be elevated.

When a believer's deat
atoms.gloss: Surah Al-Fatiha Is the Cause of Intercession

atoms.text_en:
## The Distinction Between Creator and Lord

When Allah Almighty began His utterance, He commenced with: "Praise be to Allah, Lord of the Worlds." Yet some minds might have expected: "Praise be to Allah, Creator of the Worlds." Allah praised Himself through His Lordship, not through His creation—for the station of training (lordship) exceeds creation, just as secondary perfection (kamāl thānī) sur
atoms.gloss: The Distinction Between Creator and Lord

atoms.text_en:
## The Esoteric Exegesis of "All Praise Is for Allah, Lord of the Worlds"

By Lord in this verse is meant partly the preacher and the Gate of Gates (*Bab al-Abwab*)—he who trains the forms, straightens their crookedness, and intensifies their illumination. The word *al-Alamin* (the worlds) refers to the worlds of the islands, the lords, and the hearts of heaven.

| Lord | The Worlds |
| --- | ---
atoms.gloss: The Esoteric Exegesis of "All Praise Is for Allah, Lord of the Worlds"

atoms.text_en:
## The Esoteric Exegesis of "Master of the Day of Judgment"

In Surah Al-Fatiha, Allah states that He is the Master of the Day of Judgment. Yet a day is said to be fifty thousand years—the period of the era of unveiling (*kashf*).

In the realities, the world is called the Seventy Era, and *Din* (Judgment/Religion) is the Era of Unveiling. That is, Qaʾim ʿAli is the Master of fifty thousand years
atoms.gloss: The Esoteric Exegesis of "Master of the Day of Judgment"

atoms.text_en:
## The Twenty-Five Words of Surah Al-Fatiha

Surah Al-Fatiha comprises twenty-five words, each pointing to people of exalted station. These twenty-five are: one Imam, twelve night-guides (Hujjat Layla), and twelve day-guides (Hujjat Nahari). This indicates that the Imam has established a spiritual system wherein the enlightened souls may gather near these limits and reach the Imam through the Gate
atoms.gloss: The Twenty-Five Words of Surah Al-Fatiha

atoms.text_en:
## The Seven Verses of Surah Al-Fatiha

⟪ar:سورۃ الفاتحۃ⟫The seven verses of the Qur'an are indicative of the Qur'an's meaning. Yet these verses do not indicate the true essence; rather, they indicate their veils. A veil is that which represents the true reality in the domain of manifestation. Whatever difficulties arise, they come to the veil while the true reality (that which is veiled) is prese
atoms.gloss: The Seven Verses of Surah Al-Fatiha

atoms.text_en:
## The First Perspective and the Final Perspective

Surah Al-Fatiha is a summary of the entire Qur'an. The Qur'an refers to the Qaʾim of the Hour, and Surah Al-Fatiha refers to ʿAli. In another sense, the Qur'an refers to ʿAli, and Surah Al-Fatiha refers to the Imam of the Age. ʿAli ibn Abi Talib is the first of all the Imams of the veils, and ʿAli ibn Abi Talib is himself a veil.

| Qur'an | Sura
atoms.gloss: The First Perspective and the Final Perspective

atoms.text_en:
## The Point of Ba and the Point of Nun in Surah Al-Fatiha

Surah Al-Fatiha begins with the point of *Ba* and ends with the point of *Nun*. The point of *Ba* is Amir al-Muʾminin; the letter *Ba* is the Lord of the Universe. (*Ba* and its point are distinct—for instance, remove the points of *Shin*, and it becomes *Sin*; remove the points of *Zhal*, and it becomes *Dal*; but remove the point of *Ba
atoms.gloss: The Point of Ba and the Point of Nun in Surah Al-Fatiha

atoms.text_en:
## The Meanings of Surah Al-Ikhlas

The good word reaches God, and the good deed takes away the word. Just as the formula of sincerity—*kalimah al-ikhlas* (the word of attestation, "There is no god but Allah")—constitutes the beginning of religion, so Surah Al-Ikhlas represents its conclusion. According to the decree of the All-Wise, it is necessary that the beginning and end of religion should co

## Surah Al-Ikhlas as One-Third of the Qur'an

The Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) said, "Reciting Surah Al-Ikhlas three times is equivalent to reciting the entire Qur'an." This utterance alludes to the three complete cycles within the 3,000-year period. The entire period of the Qur'an comprises the Qur'an in its entirety, in which three complete forms are established. Every com
atoms.gloss: Surah Al-Ikhlas as One-Third of the Qur'an

atoms.text_en:
## The Companions of the Two Weeks and Surah Al-Ikhlas

The first week among the seven weeks of the Imams is supreme in virtue, constituting the week of *ʿItmāʾ* (Concealment). Furthermore, the seventh personage of each week is the *Ṣāḥib al-Asbuʿ* (Master of the Week), whose station exceeds that of the other Imams of his cycle. Among these seven companions, four possess virtue of even greater mag
atoms.gloss: The Companions of the Two Weeks and Surah Al-Ikhlas

atoms.text_en:
## The Letters and Diacritical Points of Surah Al-Ikhlas

Surah Al-Ikhlas contains ten diacritical points and forty-seven letters, yielding a total of fifty-seven. This points to the existence of a person in the *daʿwa* (the summons) who embodies a comprehensiveness spanning 57,000 years. The *Qāʾim* of the Epoch of Unveiling (*kashf*) gathers the forms of 50,000 years, and the *Qāʾim* of the Seve
atoms.gloss: The Letters and Diacritical Points of Surah Al-Ikhlas

atoms.text_en:
## Surah Al-Ikhlas and Ali ibn Abi Talib

In a profound sense, *Amīr al-Muʾminīn* (the Commander of the Faithful), Ali ibn Abi Talib, is also an exemplar of this Surah. Ali said: "My relation to the ten commandments is established through the Surah of Oneness, and I am the embodiment of this Surah; every 10,000 years a form appears, and I am within it."[^cite-9]

### Qul Hu Allahu al-Wāḥid: "Say:

atoms.gloss: If Patience Were a Man, He Would Be a Righteous Man

Even in lawful things, the ascetic chooses the most humble. The believer does not shrink from Allah's blessings; rather, he partakes of them only according to his need. # Purity Statement



## An Account of Ṭahāra (Ritual Purity)

Impurity arises from impurity itself. The transition from a state of impurity to a state of purity is called ṭahāra (ritual purity). Since humanity comprises both body and soul, the purification of the soul must accompany the purification of the body. External purity is achieved through the completion of the prescribed ablutions; inner

Purity concerns the cleansing of seven organs, of which four are obligatory and three are sunna (customary) acts. Each ablution act signifies a range of spiritual and physical stations:

## Water, Buckets, and Vessels (Practical Rules)

When a worshipper wishes to perform ablution for prayer, he draws water from a river or well. The river water exemplifies the manifest knowledge of the Imam of Truth—knowledge unchanged by any hand and flowing freely to all creatures, both common and distinguished. Well water represents the inner, hidden knowledge of the Imam, reserved for those al

Water's essential quality is that it remains pure in itself and purifies others. Pure water requires no purification of its own. God purifies bodily impurity through water and removes the impurity of the mind—ignorance—through knowledge. Purification from the impurity of ignoran

The sky is the source from which water flows. Water possesses the remarkable quality of remaining pure in essence while perpetually descending from the higher realm (sky) to the lower (earth). # Purification from Impurity



## The Najāsa (Impurity) of the Disbelievers and Polytheists

In this world, one frequently encounters those who reject faith (kāfir) and polytheists (mushrik) who possess admirable outward character and speak in measured, pleasant tones. ## The Root of Impurities — the Mothers of Sins (Ummahāt al-Maʿāṣī)

Prayer and ablution have been conjoined by divine law. Through their combined performance, the believer eliminates three cardinal spiritual impurities whose very existence generates all manner of defilement: arrogance (⟪ar:کبر⟫), greed (⟪ar:حرس⟫), and envy (⟪ar:حسد⟫). To approach the state of prayer after ablution signifies enter

## Purification from Najāsa (Procedural Rules)

Impurity enters when we lend ear to Satan's summons (daʿwa) in the spiritual realm. No matter how many times a garment is laundered, cleanliness is achieved only in the place where impurity adhered; the already-clean portions remain unaffected. Similarly, when we heed the Iblīsī call and persist in it, various spiritual evils contaminate our souls. F

Ḥamīd al-Dīn al-Kirmānī (may Allāh elevate his station), in his *Risālat al-Wadīʾa*, states: A person who dwells in human form yet fails to recognize the defects of his soul has become more contemptible than ⟪ar:اھبط من القرود والخنازیر⟫ (lower than the apes and swine).

The Holy Prophet (ṣallā Allāhu ʿalayhi wa-ālihi) said to ʿAlī (ʿalayhi al-salām): "Whosoever does not fulfill the etiquette of the lavatory and does not know these observances has neither known thee nor me." atoms.gloss: The Twelve Etiquettes of the Lavatory

Maulana ʿAlī (a.s.) teaches that it is customary to begin with cleansing the private areas and to complete this action thoroughly. To neglect combining the two outlets together is disliked (makrūh), as is the use of the right hand; yet if necessity or particular circumstance compels one t
atoms.gloss: The Inner Meaning of the Pillars of Wudu

The foremost obligation in ablution is to wash both hands after one has cleansed the private areas, whether the hands are visibly soiled or apparently clean. Thereafter, the water vessel should be positioned at one's right side. One is commanded to place the right hand into the vessel and to wash both hands with water, using the right hand as the principal agent. Th

Muhammad al-Muṣṭafā (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) declared: "It is not permissible to purify oneself without intention." Before forming one's intention, it is obligatory to recite *Bismillāh ar-Raḥmān ar-Raḥīm* (In the name of Allah, the Most Compassionate, the Most Merciful).

Rinsing the mouth and pouring water into the nose are not reckoned among the pillars of ablution, inasmuch as Allah has not mentioned them explicitly in the Qur'an. atoms.gloss: Taʾwīl of Mouth-Rinsing and Use of the Miswāk

## Taʾwīl of Rinsing Water Through the Nose

After rinsing the mouth, water is poured into the nose—that is, water is drawn directly into both nostrils and then cleansed with the little finger of the left hand.

This teaches that the Summoner makes clear to the respondent: when you have acquired the knowledge of the Proof (mouth), do not associate this knowledge with the Proof alone, nor imagine t
atoms.gloss: Taʾwīl of Rinsing Water Through the Nose

## Taʾwīl of Washing the Face

The Imams (a.s.) have commanded that the face be washed from the forehead to the chin on both sides (extending to the ears) after the rinsing and nasal irrigation. One must wash thoroughly with water and rub the face with both hands.

Moreover, it has been emphasized that abundant water be applied to the eyes. The Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) sa

## The Five Senses of the Face (in the Taʾwīl)

This means that Allah can be known through the five sacred limits of religion, which in one sense are the Five Exalted Ones (Khamsat al-Athār), and in another sense are the Speaker, the Foundation, the Imam, the Proof, and the Summoner.

Thus the status of sight (basīrah) exemplifies the Intellect (al-ʿAql). If there is no obstruction before it, the
atoms.gloss: The Five Senses of the Face (in the Taʾwīl)

## Washing the Hands Up to the Elbows

When performing ablution, one should wash the right hand up to and including the elbows, then the left hand, the right foot first, and then the left foot. If one washes the left hand first and then the right, one must wash the left hand again. The Imams (a.s.) have commanded that the limb to which Allah has assigned priority in ablution should not be postpone
atoms.gloss: Washing the Hands Up to the Elbows

atoms.text_en:
## Wiping the Head, Ears, and Neck

One is commanded to wipe the front and back portions of the head. When wiping, one should first begin from the crown of the head and draw the fingers toward the forehead hair. Then, lifting the fingers, place them again upon the crown and draw them toward the back of the head. Then wipe both the inner and outer parts of both ears, and finally wipe the neck once.
atoms.gloss: Wiping the Head, Ears, and Neck

Spiritual aspiration is a garden in the heavens. Fragrances are created from exceedingly subtle and pure essences. Because of their delicate nature, when one inhales them, the body need not labor to convert them into energy and vital force. atoms.gloss: The Fragrance and Call of the Garden

A woman's menstruation (*ḥayḍ*) is blood that emerges from her own flesh. This unclean blood flows from her body once every lunar month, except when she becomes pregnant. Allāh has granted menstruation to women in order to make manifest the distinction between the imperfect and the perfect. No matter how ardently a woman strives, she cannot pray for thirty days of the month. Yet a man, who is perfect in comparison, can pray for all thirty days.[^cite-7]

Thus on the night of the Hijra, when Maulānā ʿAlī lay upon the Prophet's bed to divert the assassins, his sleep constituted an act of worship—more meritorious than the night vigils of others—because it was performed to please Allah and His Messenger. The mosque signifies the Dawat-e-Sharif (the Noble Summons), and in precisely the same sense, the place of necessity also signifies the Dawat-e-Sharif. In one sense, the mosque refers to the Imam (upon him be peace), for just as worshippers gather in a mosque, so do the followers gather around the Imam.

Whoever enters the mosque should place his right foot forward first.

**Inner Meaning**

There are three appointed times established for each prayer. The interval set aside for the performance of each prayer consists of three distinct periods, each corresponding to a fixed temporal and spiritual station:

| Early Hours | Middle Time | Final Time |

The appointed times of prayer surpass the obligatory prayers (*farāʾiḍ*) in their significance, for prayer ascends from us toward the Divine, while the times of prayer descend to us from the Divine. During these sacred hours, particular openings manifest themselves—stations (*maqāmāt*) from which the luminous chapters (*sūrāt*) of morning radiance (*al-Ishrāq*) receive and accept

All of Allah's systems operate in circular motion. Human existence itself unfolds within a circle—the space circumscribed by the span of one's extended arms. All that one accomplishes occurs within this circle. Should the circle be unfolded into a straight line, it becomes what is termed "the straight path" (*al-ṣirāṭ al-mustaqīm*

# Examples of Rak'ahs of Prayer

*Source: KASHKOLE, Binder 26 (Dua'im al-Islam: Prayer), Chapter 98. 20 topics. Language: Urdu, rendered into English.*

## Esoteric Interpretation of Prayer Categories

Prayer is fundamentally an act of drawing near to one's superior—a movement of the soul toward higher stations of knowledge and being. Every Imam, in his station, embodies four divine realities that correspond precisely to the four words of the Basmala: "In the name of God, the Merciful, the Compassionate" (⟪ar:بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

## Examples of Supererogatory Prayers

There are six supererogatory prayers, each of which embodies a profound spiritual principle. The supererogatory (tatauwwuʿ) is also called the voluntary (nāfila), signifying "descendant of the descendant"—a term the Maʿzūn uses to denote the successor of the Speaker, just as the Speaker succeeds his spiritual father, the Proof. Within this category fall nine

## Examples of the Five Prayers

The Prophet declared: "I have been given five prayers as a gift, and what no prophet before me received are these five prayers, five times, which rest upon five principles and three categories." The five daily prayers exemplify the five Possessors of Determination (Ūlū l-ʿAzm) whose Qibla (direction) and Law (Sharīʿa) are unified: Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus, and M

## The Descending Order of Prayer Rak'ahs

The divine prescriptions for prayer were revealed in five stages. In Mecca, thirty-two rak'ahs were prescribed in four periods; in Medina, thirty-one additional cycles were revealed, bringing the total to fifty-one.

### Thirty-Two Rak'ahs of Mecca

atoms.text_en:
## The Limits of Prayer

The Messenger of God said: "The limits of prayer are just as there are limits to a house." Whoever recognizes these limits and performs prayer according to their true nature and proper conditions has completed the prayer and has not broken it. The limits (ḥudūd) of prayer are seven, equal to the number of the seven Imams and seven Proofs. Of these, four are obligatory duti

## Justifications of Prayer Rak'ahs

There are in total fifty-one rak'ahs of prayer, of which seventeen are obligatory. In addition, there are thirty-four rak'ahs that are recommended or supererogatory—double the obligatory—divided into three categories: obligatory (full obligation), recommended, and supererogatory.

## Examples of the Noon Prayer

In the religion of Islam, the Holy Prophet was the first to establish the Noon Prayer (Ṣalāt al-Ẓuhr) as the "apparent" (ẓāhir) prayer. Its time falls after the sun passes its zenith, for prayer is not offered at the moment of zenith. Prayer exemplifies the summons (daʿwa), and the zenith indicates the rulings which the Speaker removes from previous revealed laws. P

## The Noon Prayer on Friday

In the spiritual realm, Mary established two eyes: one noble and one low. From your noble gaze, a congregation was formed; from your lower gaze, another congregation was formed. Then they were taken to the two gatherings.

## Examples of the Afternoon Prayer

The four rak'ahs of the Afternoon Prayer (Ṣalāt al-ʿAsr) rest upon the Master of the Standing (Sāḥib al-Qiyāma). Prayer exemplifies the summons; since there is no summons after the Qa'im (al-Qāʾim), there is no other prayer after the Afternoon Prayer. Prayers must therefore be shortened. With the appearance of the Qa'im, one epoch closes and a new account begin

## Examples of the Even, Odd, and Sitting Prayers

Whoever prays the Even Prayer for forty days, God will transform it into truth, even if falsehood emanates from his mouth.

The Even and Odd Prayers together comprise three rak'ahs, followed by two rak'ahs of the Sitting Prayer. The three rak'ahs are composed of three chapters of our Sovereign the Imam Muhammad ibn Ismaʿīl: our Sovereign ʿAbdullāh

## Examples of the Dawn Prayer

### Illuminations

It is the noblest of all times, and at that hour mercy comes most abundantly while material things diminish. The light opens seventy minutes before sunrise. At first it opens dimly, then gradually intensifies, then decreases. All of this rests upon the summons of our Sovereign the Imam al-Mahdī. The first two recommended rak'ahs exemplify his stan

## Justifications for Combining Prayers

The Prophet exemplifies all the day's prayers (from Dawn to Evening) because he stood firm in the manifest summons. In the same way, the night prayers are attributed to the Prophet as the chapter of the Messenger of God, for he prayed inwardly.

In one circle, the Even exemplifies the Prophet himself, for the first Even Prayer is offered when he rises in the morning, just as Islam began with the Prophet. There are three rak'ahs in the Even and Odd Prayer.

## Qunut of Even and Fajr

Of the seventeen obligatory rak'ahs, ten rak'ahs before the testimony are most excellent. The two rak'ahs of the Dawn Prayer are the finest, and the second of these two surpasses the first. The rak'ah in which Qunut is recited possesses a higher rank. For this reason, the Odd is the highest of the Even Prayer, for it contains Qunut.
