## Shahr Ramaḍān Is ʿAlī (the Esoteric Identification)

Ramaḍān bears among the divine names and stands as the month wherein Allāh revealed the Qur'ān. Yet this truth, spoken plainly by the surface of the text, conceals a deeper reality. The Almighty proclaims:

> ⟪quran 2:185⟫
> ⟪ar:‏شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أُنزِلَ فِيهِ ٱلْقُرْآنُ هُدًۭى لِّلنَّاسِ وَبَيِّنَتٍۢ مِّنَ ٱلْهُدَىٰ وَٱلْفُرْقَانِ ۚ
atoms.gloss: Shahr Ramaḍān Is ʿAlī (the Esoteric Identification)

atoms.text_en:
## The Ninth Month of the Year — The Asās (Foundation, i.e., Imam ʿAlī)

Ramaḍān stands as the finest month of the year, and the fast is commanded solely within it—a distinction reflecting a profound truth. Of the twelve Ḥudūd Allāh (the Limits of Allāh) established by the Prophet, only the Asās (Foundation) carries the rank of obligatory concealment upon all believers. In this manner, the Foundat
atoms.gloss: The Ninth Month of the Year — The Asās (Foundation, i.e., Imam ʿAlī)

atoms.text_en:
## The Ninth Month as the Symbolic Embodiment (Mamthūl) of the Prophet

In another circle of meaning, the month of Ramaḍān, being the ninth month, stands as the symbolic embodiment (*mamthūl*) of Muḥammad Mustaфā (may the blessings and peace of Allāh be upon him and his family), for the Prophet completed nine *dahr* (eternal cycles). Observe the progression of the Messengers:

| **Messenger** | **
atoms.gloss: The Ninth Month as the Symbolic Embodiment (Mamthūl) of the Prophet

atoms.text_en:
## The Symbolic Embodiments (Mamthūlāt) of the Thirty Days

The month of Ramaḍān encompasses precisely thirty days—neither fewer nor more. The Prophet affirmed: ⟪ar-quote:ماَ تَمَّ شَعباَنُ وَ مَا نَقَصَ رَمَضَان⟫—"Sha'bān shall never be complete, and Ramaḍān shall never be deficient." That is, Ramaḍān eternally comprises exactly thirty days, while Sha'bān remains always twenty-nine. The esoteric
atoms.gloss: The Symbolic Embodiments (Mamthūlāt) of the Thirty Days

atoms.text_en:
## The Religious Sun and Moon (Shams and Qamar of the Dīn)

All phenomena bear two aspects: the outward and the inward. Just as the physical sun illuminates the material universe, so in the religious sphere exists a sun whose light sustains the spiritual cosmos.

### Sun: The Messenger of Allāh

⟪ar:رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ آلہ وسلم⟫ is the sun of religion. Just as the visible sun stands supreme an
atoms.gloss: The Religious Sun and Moon (Shams and Qamar of the Dīn)

atoms.text_en:
## The Expiation of One Broken Fast Is Sixty Fasts

Eating, drinking, and gathering of sperm break the outward fast. The sperm enters the woman and, through conception, yields the birth of a human being. 

The esoteric meaning operates thus: The Mustajibb (Respondent) has taken the covenant (*ʿahd*) to acquire no knowledge from the apparent people and to divulge none of the knowledge of taʾwīl and
atoms.gloss: The Expiation of One Broken Fast Is Sixty Fasts

atoms.text_en: # Al-Kawakib al-Dariyah

## The Blessings (Barakāt) of the Month of Ramaḍān

The city of Ramaḍān possesses manifold dimensions of meaning. In one circle of interpretation, the month signifies Ramaḍān itself as a supreme reality ⟪ar:عین عظیمۃ⟫. In another circle, it refers to the Prophet (peace be upon him). In yet another, it designates the entire period of Muḥammad. Because of the nobility both of this month and of the p
atoms.gloss: The Blessings (Barakāt) of the Month of Ramaḍān

atoms.text_en:
## The Apportionment of Sustenance (Rizq) and Life-Span (ʿUmr)

When the city of Ramaḍān arrived, Maulānā ʿAlī (upon him be peace) would say to his children: "Exert yourselves with your souls, ⟪ar-quote:فَأَجھِدُ نُفُوسَکُم فَأِنَّ فِیہِ تَقسِیمُ الاَرزَاق وَتَوقِیتُ الآجاَل وَیَکِتبُ وَفدَ اللّٰہِ اَلَّذِینَ یَفِدُّونَ عَلَیہ⟫ for in this month is apportioned the sustenance of all, the terms of a
atoms.gloss: The Apportionment of Sustenance (Rizq) and Life-Span (ʿUmr)

atoms.text_en:
## The Reward of a Single Good Deed in Shahrullāh

Allāh the Almighty has declared that in this month, whoever performs a good deed receives its reward seventy times over. That is, one who draws near to Allāh through the righteous deeds of the age of Muḥammad (peace and blessings be upon him) becomes as one who has progressed from the realm of the Mustajīb (the one who responds—the first spiritual
atoms.gloss: The Reward of a Single Good Deed in Shahrullāh

atoms.text_en:
## The Reward of Hosting an Ifṭār in Shahrullāh

Whoever causes a believer to break his fast in this month—Allāh will forgive all his sins and liberate his neck from Hell. That is, if a person of higher rank liberates one who was bound in fasting (while fasting) and causes him to become manifest (muẓhar), then Allāh forgives all the sins of that higher rank and liberates his neck, elevating his st
atoms.gloss: The Reward of Hosting an Ifṭār in Shahrullāh

atoms.text_en:
## The Thirty Days and Nights of Shahrullāh

The thirty fasts of the duties of the city of Allāh are ordained for the thirty Imāms who withdrew into concealment for reasons of spiritual protection (taqiyya). These are the thirty Imāms—from ⟪ar:مولانا علی الطیب⟫ Maulānā ʿAlī al-Ṭayyib (the 21st Imām) to ⟪ar:مہدی آخر الزمان⟫ the Mahdi of the end of time (the 50th Imām)—though they are not counted in
atoms.gloss: The Thirty Days and Nights of Shahrullāh

atoms.text_en:
## The Three Decades of Shahrullāh—Mercy, Forgiveness, Liberation from Fire

The thirty days of the month of Allāh are divided into three decades (dihiqq). These three decades correspond to three sequences of Imāms:

| Mercy | From Maulānā Ḥasan to Maulānā Muḥammad ibn Ismāʿīl |
| --- | --- |
| Forgiveness | From Maulānā Muḥammad ibn Ismāʿīl to Maulānā ʿAlī ibn al-Ṭayyib |
| Liberation from Fire |
atoms.gloss: The Three Decades of Shahrullāh—Mercy, Forgiveness, Liberation from Fire

atoms.text_en:
## Thalāthat Sufarāʾ—The Three 'Envoys' of the Three Decades

Beyond these interpretations, the three decades of the city of Allāh are indicative of three stations (maqāmāt) which are unparalleled throughout the seventy ages—which are the three celestial journeys (safar).

| Mercy | Forgiveness | Liberation from Fire |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Shaybat al-Ḥamd | ʿImrān | Dhū al-Qarnayn |
| ʿAbdul Muṭṭ
atoms.gloss: Thalāthat Sufarāʾ—The Three 'Envoys' of the Three Decades

atoms.text_en:
## The Importance of Maghfira (Forgiveness) in Shahrullāh

The Messenger of Allāh (upon him be blessings and peace) once declared: "O people, Gabriel (upon him be peace) has just come to me and extended his greeting." He said, "⟪ar:محمد (ﷺ)⟫ Whoever finds the month of Ramaḍān and is not forgiven and dies, let him be cast into Hell—and may Allāh remove him." You say Āmīn. So I said Āmīn!"[^cite-8]
atoms.gloss: The Importance of Maghfira (Forgiveness) in Shahrullāh

atoms.text_en:
## Three Things That Bring God's Pleasure

Imām Jaʿfar as-Ṣādiq (upon him be peace) has stated that there are three things pleasing to Allāh:

### Vigil of the Night with Prayer

That is, to remain awake in the seventy ages with ritual prayers. The Tahajjud prayer (night vigil) is connected to the true prayer, and this can only be performed by one who receives the Holy Spirit from his Imām (upon h
atoms.gloss: Three Things That Bring God's Pleasure

atoms.text_en:
## The Prophet's Bedding and Waistband (in Ramaḍān)

It is narrated that the Messenger of Allāh (upon him be blessings and peace) would perform three acts during the final days of the city of Allāh, in which the Night of Power occurs:

| 1 | He would wrap his bed |
| --- | --- |
| 2 | He would tighten his waistband |
| 3 | He would wake his family on the 23rd night and sprinkle water upon the face
atoms.gloss: The Prophet's Bedding and Waistband (in Ramaḍān)

atoms.text_en:
## Yā Umma Abīhā—'O Mother of Her Father' (Fāṭima's Epithet)

The Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him) would address Maulānā ⟪ar-quote:یا ام ابیھا⟫ Fāṭima thus: "O mother of her father." The mother of the Holy Prophet was Ḥazrat Āminah, who became the Gateway of Gateways (Bāb al-Abwāb) of Maulānā ʿAbdullāh and the heart of the Holy Prophet. And he gave this temple of thine to Maulānā Fāṭima.
atoms.gloss: Yā Umma Abīhā—'O Mother of Her Father' (Fāṭima's Epithet)

The Night of Farewell (⟪ar:لیلۃ الوداع⟫, *laylat al-wadāʿ*) and the Night of Fast-Breaking (⟪ar:لیلۃ الفتر⟫, *laylat al-fiṭr*) mark a profound threshold in sacred time. These nights serve as witnesses and guides, pointing towards their spiritual correlates in the cycle of revelation. The example of this correspondence is the Mahdi, who
atoms.gloss: Ṣalāt al-Wadāʿ — The Farewell Prayer (the Night of Names)

## The Four Blessed Nights of the Year

Commander of the Faithful ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib (upon him be peace) said: "I love that a person devote himself to the remembrance and worship of Allah during these four nights in a year."

| 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| First of Rajab | Mid-Shaʿbān | Eid al-Fiṭr | Eid al-Aḍḥā |

atoms.gloss: The Four Blessed Nights of the Year

Beautiful clothing should be worn on ʿĪd, and one should bathe and apply fragrance, for fine garments are woven from the refined essence of waste, and perfume and bathwater exemplify ascending substances. When the Qa'im—the Establisher upon whose name blessings descend—manifests, the liv
atoms.gloss: Yawm al-ʿĪdayn — The Day of the Two ʿĪds

When the Messenger of Allah returned after the ʿĪd prayer, he did not depart by the path on which he had come from his house to the mosque. The path from house to mosque signifies the *daʿwa* (the summons, the invitation) and contains the virtues of those who were trusted in the hidden period. The path taken in return from mosque
atoms.gloss: Why Did the Prophet Return from ʿĪd Prayer by a Different Route?

The night of Eid al-Fiṭr is the second pilgrimage of the Mahdi to the End of Time, which shall endure throughout the Mahdi's era and make special people aware of his station. The day of Eid al-Fiṭr refers to our Lord the Mahdi.

| Night of Eid al-Fiṭr | Day of Eid al-Fiṭr |

The day of Eid al-Aḍḥā is celebrated as the manifestation of our Lord the Qa'im—the Establisher upon whose name blessings descend. We call the Qa'im "*Qa'im al-Qiyāma*" (the Establisher of the Standing, the Rising) when they merge with the First Eye and the Eye of the World to Come. The night of Eid al-Aḍḥā exemplifies our Lord the Ma

In the first *rakʿa*, the *qunut* (supplication) is recited five times with six *takbīrs* (magnifications: "Allāhu Akbar—Allah is Greatest"), and in the second *rakʿa*, *qunut* is recited four times with five *takbīrs*. *Ṣalāt* al-ʿĪdayn (ʿĪd prayers) or *Ṣalāt* al-Yawm al-Jumʿa (the Friday prayer) cannot be performed unless at least five people stand behind the Imam in a city.

**Mawlānā Fāṭima**

In a higher sense, Eid al-Fiṭr and Eid al-Aḍḥā refer to two mighty stations—the Mahdi of the End of Time and the Qa'im—upon whose name blessings descend:

At the end of each cosmic cycle (*dawr*, a period) and each prophetic utterance (*nuṭq*, the speaking), there shall also appear a Mahdi and a Qa'im. These prayers signify them. Th
atoms.gloss: ʿĪd as a Return — The Spiritual Significance of 'Coming Back'

atoms.gloss: Mālik ibn al-Ḥārith al-Ashtar al-Nakhaʿī — Companion-General of Imam ʿAlī

atoms.text_en:
## Uways al-Qaranī — The Yemeni Devotee of the Prophet

A resident of Syria, Ḥaḍrat Uwais al-Qaranī (*Uways of Qarn*) harbored such a consuming love for the Holy Prophet that his heart could scarcely contain it. He yearned with every fiber to behold the Prophet's face, yet his mother—aged and blind—forbade him to journey to Madina. The distance became unbearable torment; he questioned every travel
atoms.gloss: Uways al-Qaranī — The Yemeni Devotee of the Prophet

## Sayyidnā al-Muʾayyad al-Shīrāzī (may God elevate his rank) — Fatimid Chief Daʿwa Officer

The succession of Summoners (*du'āt*, plural of dāʿī) commenced with Sayyidina Dhuʿayb ibn Mūsa and has continued to the present day, producing fifty-two (52) successive preachers—each authorized by the Imam to proclaim the summons (*daʿwa*, the invitation to the faith). Among these, Sayyidina Hibatallāh i
atoms.gloss: Sayyidnā al-Muʾayyad al-Shīrāzī (may God elevate his rank) — Fatimid Chief Daʿwa Officer

## Al-Muʾayyad's Verses to Imām al-Mustanṣir bi-Llāh and the Imām's Reply

At last, unable to contain the burning of his heart, he gave voice to his sorrow in verse. atoms.arabic: مَن عَرَفَ نَفسَہُ فَقَدْ عَرَفَ رَبُّہُ

atoms.text_en: I see that intellects are of two types... internal (physical) and external (spiritual). The internal (physical) intellect cannot benefit until it is combined with the external (spiritual) intellect. Just as (light of the) sun cannot be beneficial if the light (sight) of the eye is missing.
atoms.gloss: I see that intellects are of two types... internal (physical) and external (spiritual). The internal (physical) intellect cannot benefit until it is combined with the external (spiritual) intellect. Just as (light of the) sun cannot be beneficial if the light (sight) of the eye is missing.
atoms.arabic: رَأيتُ العقلَ عَقلَين ... فمَطبُوعٌ وَمَسمُوعُ۔ وَلا يَنفَعُ مَسْمُوعٌ إِذَا لم يَكُ مَطبُوع۔ كَما لا تَنفَعُ الشَّمْسُ وَضَوءَ العَينِ مَمْنُوع

atoms.text_en: Do not reflect on Allah, instead reflect on the creations of Allah.
atoms.gloss: Do not reflect on Allah, instead reflect on the creations of Allah.
atoms.arabic: لا تَفَکُّر فِی اللہ وَلکِنْ تَفَکُّر فِی خَلْقِ اللہ

atoms.text_en: SHIRK (Associating partnership with Allah) is more hidden in my nation, than the movement of an ant in a dark night on a black stone.
atoms.gloss: SHIRK (Associating partnership with Allah) is more hidden in my nation, than the movement of an ant in a dark night on a black stone.
atoms.arabic: الشِّرکُ فِی أُمَّتِی أَخفٰی مِن سَیرِ النَّمَلِ فِی لَیلَۃِ الظُّلمَۃِ عَلی سَخْرَۃِ السَّوْدَا

atoms.text_en: Indeed, TAWHEED is to recognize the spiritual and physical definitions (entities) and their precedence; and to admit that indeed each and every entity is alone in his rank, and does not share it with anyone else.
atoms.gloss: Indeed, TAWHEED is to recognize the spiritual and physical definitions (entities) and their precedence; and to admit that indeed each and every entity is alone in his rank, and does not share it with anyone else.
atoms.arabic: اَنَّ اَلتَّوحِیدُ ھُوَ مَعرِفَتِ الحُدُودِ الرُّوحاَنِیَّۃ وَ الجِسمَانِیَّۃ العاَلِیَۃ وَالدَّانِیَۃ وَالأعتَرَاف بِأَنَّ کُلَّ حدٍّ مِنھُم واَحِداً فِی مَرتَبَتِہِ لاَ یُشاَرِکہُ فِیھاَ سِوَاہُ

atoms.text_en: When you attempt to reach Allah become silent
atoms.gloss: When you attempt to reach Allah become silent

atoms.text_en: We are extremely content with the distribution set by the powerful. He gave us (spiritual) knowledge and gave our enemies (physical) wealth.
atoms.gloss: We are extremely content with the distribution set by the powerful. He gave us (spiritual) knowledge and gave our enemies (physical) wealth.
atoms.arabic: رَضِینٰی قِسمَتِ الجَبَّارِ فِینا۔ لَنَا عِلْمٌ وَلی أَعْدَائِی مَالٌ

atoms.text_en: Praise is due to Allah whose worth cannot be described by speakers, whose bounties cannot be counted by calculators and whose claim (to obedience) cannot be satisfied by those who attempt to do so, whom the height of intellectual courage cannot appreciate, and the diving of understanding cannot reach; He for whose description no limit has been laid down, no eulogy exists, no time is ordained and n
atoms.gloss: Praise is due to Allah whose worth cannot be described by speakers, whose bounties cannot be counted by calculators and whose claim (to obedience) cannot be satisfied by those who attempt to do so, whom the height of intellectual courage cannot appreciate, and the diving of understanding cannot reach; He for whose description no limit has been laid down, no eulogy exists, no time is ordained and no duration is fixed.
atoms.arabic: الحَمْدُ للهِ الَّذَي لاَ يَبْلُغُ مِدْحَتَهُ القَائِلُونَ، وَلاِ يُحْصِي نَعْمَاءَهُ العَادُّونَ، ولاَ يُؤَدِّي حَقَّهُ الُمجْتَهِدُونَ، الَّذِي لاَ يُدْركُهُ بُعْدُ الهِمَمِ، وَلاَ يَنَالُهُ غَوْصُ الفِطَنِ، الَّذِي لَيْسَ لِصِفَتِهِ حَدٌّ مَحْدُودٌ، وَلاَ نَعْتٌ مَوْجُودٌ، وَلا وَقْتٌ مَعْدُودٌ، وَلا أَجَلٌ مَمْدُودٌ

atoms.text_en: [It is] A place that no eye has seen, and no ear has heard, and no human heart has experienced
atoms.gloss: [It is] A place that no eye has seen, and no ear has heard, and no human heart has experienced
atoms.arabic: مَا لا عَيْنٌ رَأَتْ وَلا أُذنٌ سَمِعَت وَلا خَطَرَ عَلىٰ قَلْبِ بَشَرْ

atoms.text_en: The benefit (reward) of actions is at their conclusion
atoms.gloss: The benefit (reward) of actions is at their conclusion

atoms.text_en: Hassan and Hussain are the Imams of righteousness and their father is better than them
atoms.gloss: Hassan and Hussain are the Imams of righteousness and their father is better than them
atoms.arabic: الحَسَنُ وَ الحُسَینُ أِمَامَ حَقٍّ قَامَ أَو قَعَدَ وَ أَبُوھُمَا خَیْرٌ مِن ھُمَا

atoms.text_en: Praise be to Allah who lies inside all hidden things, and towards whom all manifest things guide. He cannot be seen by the eyes of the one who can see; and the eye which does not see him cannot deny his existence; and the heart that proves his existence cannot perceive Him. He is so lofty in his sublimity that nothing can be more sublime than him; and in closeness, he is so near that no one can be
atoms.gloss: Praise be to Allah who lies inside all hidden things, and towards whom all manifest things guide. He cannot be seen by the eyes of the one who can see; and the eye which does not see him cannot deny his existence; and the heart that proves his existence cannot perceive Him. He is so lofty in his sublimity that nothing can be more sublime than him; and in closeness, he is so near that no one can be more closer than him. His sublimity does not put him at a distance from any of his creation; nor does his closeness and nearness to creation bring them on an equal level to him. He has not revealed or disclosed the true extents of his divine qualities to the intellects; nor has he prevented us from securing the essential knowledge about him. He is such, that all signs of existence stand witness for Him - to the extent that even the mind that denies him, is ultimately forced to testify for him. Allah is sublime beyond what is described by those who liken him to things, or those strive against him. For he is sublime and exalted beyond comprehension.
atoms.arabic: الْحَمْدُ للهِ الَّذِي بَطَنَ خَفِيَّاتِ الاْمُورِ، وَدَلَّتْ عَلَيْهِ أَعْلاَمُ الظُّهُورِ۔ وَامْتَنَعَ عَلَى عَيْنِ الْبَصِيرِ; فَلاَ عَيْنُ مَنْ لَمْ يَرَهُ تُنْكِرُه۔ وَلاَ قَلْبُ مَنْ أَثْبَتَهُ يُبْصِرُهُ۔ سَبَقَ فِي الْعُلُوِّ فَلاَ شَيءَ أَعْلَى مِنْهُ۔ وَقَرُبَ فِي الدُّنُوِّ فَلاَ شَيْءَ أَقْرَبُ مِنْهُ۔ فَلاَ اسْتِعْلاَؤُهُ بِاعَدَهُ عَنْ شَيْء مِنْ خَلْقِهِ۔ وَلاَ قُرْبُهُ سَوَاهُمْ في المَكَانِ بِه۔ لَمْ يُطْلِعِ الْعُقُولَ عَلَى تَحْدِيدِ صِفَتِهِ۔ ولَمْ يَحْجُبْهَا عَنْ وَاجِبِ مَعْرِفِتِهِ۔ فَهُوَ الَّذِي تَشْهَدُ لَهُ أَعْلاَمُ الْوُجُودِ، عَلَى إِقْرَارِ قَلْبِ ذِي الْجُحُودِ۔ تَعَالَى اللهُ عَمَّا يَقولُ الْمُشَبِّهُونَ بِهِ وَالْجَاحِدُونَ لَهُ - عُلوّاً كَبِيراً۔

atoms.text_en: The messenger of a man is his intellect
atoms.gloss: The messenger of a man is his intellect

atoms.text_en: Then Allah sent His Messengers and series of His prophets towards them to get them to fulfill the pledges of His creation. and to remind them of his bounties and blessings, and to exhort them by preaching, in order to unearth for them the buried treasures of the intellects.
atoms.gloss: Then Allah sent His Messengers and series of His prophets towards them to get them to fulfill the pledges of His creation. and to remind them of his bounties and blessings, and to exhort them by preaching, in order to unearth for them the buried treasures of the intellects.
atoms.arabic: فَبَعَثَ فِيهمْ رُسُلَهُ، وَوَاتَرَ إِلَيْهِمْ أَنْبِياءَهُ لِيَسْتَأْدُوهُمْ مِيثَاقَ فِطْرَتِهِ۔ وَيُذَكِّرُوهُمْ مَنْسِيَّ نِعْمَتِهِ، وَيَحْتَجُّوا عَلَيْهِمْ بَالتَّبْلِيغِ، وَيُثِيرُوا لَهُمْ دَفَائِنَ الْعُقُولِ

atoms.text_en: Allah collected from hard, soft, sweet and sour earth, clay which He dripped in water till it got pure, and kneaded it with moisture till it became gluey. From it He carved an image with curves, joints, limbs and segments. He solidified it till it dried up for a fixed time and a known duration. Then He blew into it out of His Spirit whereupon it took the pattern of a human being with mind that gov
atoms.gloss: Allah collected from hard, soft, sweet and sour earth, clay which He dripped in water till it got pure, and kneaded it with moisture till it became gluey. From it He carved an image with curves, joints, limbs and segments. He solidified it till it dried up for a fixed time and a known duration. Then He blew into it out of His Spirit whereupon it took the pattern of a human being with mind that governs him, intelligence which he makes use of, limbs that serve him, organs that change his position, sagacity that differentiates between truth and falsehood
atoms.arabic: ثُمَّ جَمَعَ سُبْحَانَهُ مِنْ حَزْنِ الاْرْضِ وَسَهْلِهَا، وَعَذْبِهَا وَسَبَخِهَا۔ تُرْبَةً سَنَّهَا بالمَاءِ حَتَّى خَلَصَتْ۔ وَلاَطَهَا بِالبَلَّةِ حَتَّى لَزَبَتْ۔ فَجَبَلَ مِنْها صُورَةً ذَاتَ أَحْنَاءَ وَوُصُولَ، وَأَعْضَاءَ وَفُصُول۔ أَجْمَدَهَا حَتَّى اسْتَمْسَكَتْ، وَأَصْلَدَهَا حَتَّى صَلْصَلَتْ۔ لِوَقْت مَعْدُود، وَأجَل مَعْلُوم۔ ثُمَّ نَفَخَ فِيها مِنْ رُوحِهِ فَمَثُلَتْ إِنْساناً ذَا أَذْهَانُ يُجيلُهَا، وَفِكَر يَتَصَرَّفُ بِهَا، وَجَوَارِحَ يَخْتَدِمُهَا، وَأَدَوَات يُقَلِّبُهَا، وَمَعْرِفَة يَفْرُقُ بِهَا بَيْنَ الحَقِّ وَالبَاطِلِ۔

atoms.text_en: My nation will divide into 73 sects of which only one will group will be the saved ones
atoms.gloss: My nation will divide into 73 sects of which only one will group will be the saved ones

atoms.arabic: سَتَفْتَرِقُ أُمَّتِی فِی ثَلاثَۃَ وَ سَبْعِینَ فِرْقَۃً وَاحِدٌ نَاجِیَۃ

atoms.text_en: Indeed, after Allah established all his creation, he wrote on his throne, 'Indeed my mercy shall overcome my wrath'
atoms.gloss: Indeed, after Allah established all his creation, he wrote on his throne, 'Indeed my mercy shall overcome my wrath'
atoms.source_ref: hadith · mercy shall overcome my wrath
atoms.arabic: إنَّ الله لَمَّا قَضٰى الخَلق،كَتَبَ فَوقَ عَرْشُه اِنَّ رَحمَتِی تَغلِبُ غَضَبِی

atoms.text_en: Abstraction is the process of separating the ability of creating from nothingness from all creation and applying it to the creator.
atoms.gloss: Abstraction is the process of separating the ability of creating from nothingness from all creation and applying it to the creator.
atoms.arabic: وَالتَّجرِیدُ صَلبُ اِلأِلٰھِیَۃ عَن جَمِیعِ المُبدَعاَت وَالمَخلُوقاَت وَالأِثباَتُھَا لِمُبدِع

atoms.text_en: Transcendence is the process of removing all attributes of creation from the creator. It is recognizing that every attribute of nobility and dignity, and every attribute that could be constructed or derived through language or indications in the praise of the creator, do not apply to him, but to the First Intellect.
atoms.gloss: Transcendence is the process of removing all attributes of creation from the creator. It is recognizing that every attribute of nobility and dignity, and every attribute that could be constructed or derived through language or indications in the praise of the creator, do not apply to him, but to the First Intellect.
atoms.arabic: وَالتَّنزِیہ نَفِی اَلصِّفاَتُ عَن غَیبُ الغُیُوب وَالأِعتَراَف عَن جَمِیعِ الصِّفاَت الشَّرَفِ وَالجَلاَلَۃِ وَماَ یَعبَرُ بِہِ جَمِیعَ الُّغاَت وَالأِشارَۃ بِنَعُوتِ الأِلہِیَۃ وَأِنَّھاَ وَاقِعَۃٌ اِلیٰ العَقلِ اَوَّلِ

atoms.text_en: I have been commanded to fight people until they say there is no god but Allah, and Muhammad (PBUH) is his prophet,and until they establish prayer and give alms. Once once they do that, their blood and wealth are protected, except by the right of Islam, otherwise their reckoning is with Allah.
atoms.gloss: I have been commanded to fight people until they say there is no god but Allah, and Muhammad (PBUH) is his prophet,and until they establish prayer and give alms. Once once they do that, their blood and wealth are protected, except by the right of Islam, otherwise their reckoning is with Allah.
atoms.arabic: َأُمِرتُ أَنْ أُقاتِلَ النَّاسَ حَتّٰی یَشْھَدُوا أَن لا الہ الا اللہ و أنَّ محمدا رسول اللہ ؛ و یُقِیمُ الصَّلاۃَ وَ یُؤتُوا الزَّکاۃَ، فِأِذَا فَعَلُوا ذَالِکَ عَصِمُوا مِنِّی دِمائُھُمْ وَ أَموَالُھُمْ، اِلَّا بِحَقِّ الأِسْلامِ وَ حِسَابُھُمْ عَلیَ اللہِ تَعَالی

atoms.text_en: Man should call upon Allah alone to provide for all his needs, so much so that even if his shoe-lace snaps, he should pray to Allah to provide him with a shoe-lace
atoms.gloss: Man should call upon Allah alone to provide for all his needs, so much so that even if his shoe-lace snaps, he should pray to Allah to provide him with a shoe-lace
atoms.arabic: لِيَسْأَلْ أَحَدُكُمْ رَبَّهُ حَاجَتَهُ كُلَّهَا حَتَّى يَسْأَلَ شِسْعَ نَعْلِهِ إِذَا انْقَطَعَ

atoms.text_en: There is not a single one that does not have a permanent partner associated with him
atoms.gloss: There is not a single one that does not have a permanent partner associated with him
atoms.arabic: مَا مِنکُم مِن احدٍ اِلَّا قَد وُکِّلَ بِہِ قَرِینُہُ

atoms.text_en: This world is a womb for the afterlife
atoms.gloss: This world is a womb for the afterlife

atoms.text_en: Every action is infertile without the remembrance of Allah
atoms.gloss: Every action is infertile without the remembrance of Allah
atoms.arabic: کُلَّ شَئیٍ أَبْتَر بِدُونِ بِسمِ اللہ

atoms.text_en: ?My Master, I do not worship you out of a desire for your paradise; nor do I worship you out of fear of your hellfire. Instead I truly find you worthy of my worship, and for that reason alone I worship you.
atoms.gloss: ?My Master, I do not worship you out of a desire for your paradise; nor do I worship you out of fear of your hellfire. Instead I truly find you worthy of my worship, and for that reason alone I worship you.
atoms.arabic: یَا أِلھِی مَا عَبَدْتُّکَ بِشَوقِ جَنَّتِکَ وَلاَ مِنْ خَوفِ نَارِکَ بَلْ وَجَدْۃُ مُسْتَحَقّاً لِعِبَادَۃ فَلعَبَدْتُّکَ

atoms.text_en: Paradise was surrounded by distasteful things and the fire was surrounded by delightful things
atoms.gloss: Paradise was surrounded by distasteful things and the fire was surrounded by delightful things
atoms.arabic: حُفَّۃُ الجَنَّۃ بِمَکارِہْ وَحُفَّۃُ النَّارُ بِالشَّھْوَاتْ

atoms.text_en: Take yourself to account before you're taken to account.
atoms.gloss: Take yourself to account before you're taken to account.

atoms.text_en: Every religion has a characteristic, and the characteristic of my religion is modesty.
atoms.gloss: Every religion has a characteristic, and the characteristic of my religion is modesty.
atoms.source_ref: hadith · Modesty is the characteristic of Islam
atoms.arabic: لِکُلِّ دِیْنٍ خُلُق وَخُلُقَ الاِسْلام أَلحَیاءٌ

atoms.text_en: Indeed in the body there is a lump of flesh. If it is set right, then the entire body will be set right. And if it is corrupted, then the entire body will be corrupted. Indeed it is the heart.
atoms.gloss: Indeed in the body there is a lump of flesh. If it is set right, then the entire body will be set right. And if it is corrupted, then the entire body will be corrupted. Indeed it is the heart.
atoms.arabic: أِنَّ فِی الجَسَدِ مُضْغَۃ۔ أِذَا صَلُحَتْ صَلَحَ الجَسَدُ کُلُّہ وَ أِذَا فَسَدَتْ فَسَدَ الجَسَدُ کُلُّہ۔ أَلا وَھِیَ القَلْبٌ

atoms.text_en: All children of Adam will make mistakes; and the best of them are the ones who repent
atoms.gloss: All children of Adam will make mistakes; and the best of them are the ones who repent
atoms.source_ref: hadith · All children of Adam will make mistakes
atoms.arabic: كُلُّ بَنِي آدَمَ خَطَّاءٌ, وَخَيْرُ اَلْخَطَّائِينَ اَلتَّوَّابُونَ

atoms.text_en: I heard the Messenger of Allah (?) say: Allah the Almighty said: O son of Adam, so long as you call upon Me and ask of Me, I shall forgive you for what you have done, and I shall not mind. O son of Adam, were your sins to reach the clouds of the sky and were you then to ask forgiveness of Me, I would forgive you. O son of Adam, were you to come to Me with sins nearly as great as the earth and were
atoms.gloss: I heard the Messenger of Allah (?) say: Allah the Almighty said: O son of Adam, so long as you call upon Me and ask of Me, I shall forgive you for what you have done, and I shall not mind. O son of Adam, were your sins to reach the clouds of the sky and were you then to ask forgiveness of Me, I would forgive you. O son of Adam, were you to come to Me with sins nearly as great as the earth and were you then to face Me, ascribing no partner to Me, I would bring you forgiveness nearly as great as it.
atoms.source_ref: hadith · sins that fill the sky
atoms.arabic: قَالَ اللهُ تَعَالَى : يَا ابْنَ ادَمَ ، إِنَّكَ مَا دَعَوْتََنِي وَرَجَوْتَنِي ، غَفَرْتُ لَكَ عَلَى مَا كَانَ مِنْكَ وَلَا أُبالِي . يا ابْنَ ادَمَ :لَوْ بَلَغَتْ ذُنُوبُكَ عَنانَ السَّماءِ ثُم َ اسْتَغْفَرْتَني ، غَفَرْتُ لَكَ . يَا ابْنَ ادَمَ : إِنَّكَ لَوْ أَتَيْتَنِي بِقُرَابِ الأَرْضِ خَطَايا ثُمَّ لَقِيتَني لَا تُشْرِكُ بِي شَيْأً ، لَأَتيْتُكَ بِقُرَابِها مَغْفِرَةً

atoms.text_en: I am in the opinion of my slave; and I am with him when he remembers me. When he remembers me inwardly, I remember him inwardly. When he remembers me in an assembly, I remember him in a much greater assembly (the assembly of angels). When he takes a step towards me, I move an arm's length towards him. When he moves an arms length towards me, I move a yard towards him. When he walks towards me, I r
atoms.gloss: I am in the opinion of my slave; and I am with him when he remembers me. When he remembers me inwardly, I remember him inwardly. When he remembers me in an assembly, I remember him in a much greater assembly (the assembly of angels). When he takes a step towards me, I move an arm's length towards him. When he moves an arms length towards me, I move a yard towards him. When he walks towards me, I run towards him.
atoms.source_ref: hadith · I am in the opinion of my slave
atoms.arabic: أنا عِنْدَ ظَنِّ عبدي بي ، وأنا معه إذا ذكرني ، فإن ذكرني في نفسه، ذكرته في نفسي ؛ وإن ذكرني في ملإ، ذكرته في ملإ خيرٌ مِنهُم ؛ وإن تقرب إلي بِشِبْر، تقربت إليه ذِرَاعاً ؛ وإن تقرب إلي ذِرَاعاً ، تقربت إليه بَاعاً ؛ وإن أتاني يمشي، أتيته هَرَوْلة

atoms.text_en: The first thing that Allah created is the intellect. When Allah created the Intellect, He examined it. He then said to it: Step forward, so it stepped forward. Then He said: Go back! So it went back. Then he said: By My power and majesty! I did not create any creature dearer to me than you. I will not make you perfect, except in the one whom I love. Indeed, to you are my orders and prohibitions ad
atoms.gloss: The first thing that Allah created is the intellect. When Allah created the Intellect, He examined it. He then said to it: Step forward, so it stepped forward. Then He said: Go back! So it went back. Then he said: By My power and majesty! I did not create any creature dearer to me than you. I will not make you perfect, except in the one whom I love. Indeed, to you are my orders and prohibitions addressed. And for you are my rewards and retributions reserved.
atoms.arabic: أول ما خلق اللہُ عزّ وجلّ العقل۔ ثمّ قال له: أقبل! فأقبل ثمّ قال له: أدبر! فأدبر۔ ثمّ قال: وعزّتي وجلالي! ما خلقت خلقاً هو أحبٌ إلىً منك ؛ ولا أكمَلتُكِ إلاّ فِي من أُحِبٌ۔ أما إنّي إيّاك آمر وإيّاك

atoms.text_en: Islam began as a stranger, and will become a strange again towards the end of time, as it was in the beginning. There is glad tidings for the estranged
atoms.gloss: Islam began as a stranger, and will become a strange again towards the end of time, as it was in the beginning. There is glad tidings for the estranged
atoms.source_ref: hadith · Islam will be estranged; become a stranger
atoms.arabic: بَدَأَ الأِسْلامَ غَرِیْباً وَسَیَعُوْدُ غَرِیباً کَماَ بَدَأ وَطُوْبیٰ لِی الغُرَبَاء

atoms.text_en: None of you has truly attained faith until I am more beloved to him than his own father, his own child, and all of humanity combined.
atoms.gloss: None of you has truly attained faith until I am more beloved to him than his own father, his own child, and all of humanity combined.
atoms.arabic: لَا يُؤْمِنُ أَحَدُكُمْ حَتَّى أَكُونَ أَحَبَّ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ وَالِدِهِ وَوَلَدِهِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ

atoms.text_en: Everything in this world, no matter how great it sounds, is ultimately less impressive in reality. As for the Hereafter, no matter how glorious you imagine it to be, its reality will be far more magnificent.
atoms.gloss: Everything in this world, no matter how great it sounds, is ultimately less impressive in reality. As for the Hereafter, no matter how glorious you imagine it to be, its reality will be far more magnificent.
atoms.source_ref: hadith · Duniya, Akhira, Worldly Life, Hereafter
atoms.arabic: كُلُّ شَيْءٍ مِنَ الدُّنْيَا سَمَاعُهُ أَعْظَمُ مِنْ عِيَانِهِ، وَكُلُّ شَيْءٍ مِنَ الْآخِرَةِ عِيَانُهُ أَعْظَمُ مِنْ سَمَاعِهِ

atoms.text_en: I was only sent to perfect good character.
atoms.gloss: I was only sent to perfect good character.

atoms.text_en: I was sent with the tolerant way of the Hanif
atoms.gloss: I was sent with the tolerant way of the Hanif

atoms.text_en: Indeed, this prayer is not suitable for any of the speech of people. It is only for glorifying Allah, praising Him, and exalting Him
atoms.gloss: Indeed, this prayer is not suitable for any of the speech of people. It is only for glorifying Allah, praising Him, and exalting Him
atoms.arabic: إنَّ هذه الصلاة لا يَصلُحُ فيها شيءٌ من كلامِ الناس، إنما هي التَّسبيحُ والتَّحميدُ والتَّكبيرُ

atoms.text_en: You may see yourself as insignificant, but inside you lies the most incredible universe.
atoms.gloss: You may see yourself as insignificant, but inside you lies the most incredible universe.
atoms.arabic: وَ تَحْسَبُ أَنَّكَ‏ جِرْمٌ‏ صَغِيرٌ وَ فِيكَ انْطَوَى الْعَالَمُ الْأَكْبَرُ

atoms.text_en: Hold yourselves accountable before you are held accountable, and weigh your deeds before they are weighed
atoms.gloss: Hold yourselves accountable before you are held accountable, and weigh your deeds before they are weighed
atoms.arabic: حَاسِبُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ قَبْلَ أَنْ تُحَاسَبُوا، وَزِنُوا أَعْمَالَكُمْ قَبْلَ أَنْ تُوزَنُوا

atoms.text_en: There is not a single day except that the sea seeks its Lord's permission to drown the son of Adam, and the earth seeks its Lord's permission to swallow him, and the angels seek His permission to hasten and destroy him. And the Lord Almighty says: 'Leave My servant, for I know him better, since I created him from the earth. If he is your servant, then you have a right over him, but if he is My ser
atoms.gloss: There is not a single day except that the sea seeks its Lord's permission to drown the son of Adam, and the earth seeks its Lord's permission to swallow him, and the angels seek His permission to hasten and destroy him. And the Lord Almighty says: 'Leave My servant, for I know him better, since I created him from the earth. If he is your servant, then you have a right over him, but if he is My servant, then he is from Me and to Me.' By My Honor and My Majesty, if he comes to Me by night, I will accept him, and if he comes to Me by day, I will accept him. If he draws near to Me by a handspan, I will draw near to him by a cubit. If he draws near to Me by a cubit, I will draw near to him by a fathom. And if he walks to Me, I will run to him. If he seeks My forgiveness, I will forgive him. If he repents to Me, I will accept his repentance.
atoms.arabic: مَا مِنْ يَوْمٍ إِلَّا وَالْبَحْرُ يَسْتَأْذِنُ رَبَّهُ أَنْ يُغْرِقَ ابْنَ آدَمَ، وَالْأَرْضُ تَسْتَأْذِنُ رَبَّهَا أَنْ تَبْتَلِعَهُ، وَالْمَلَائِكَةُ تَسْتَأْذِنُهُ أَنْ تُعَاجِلَهُ وَتُهْلِكَهُ۔ وَالرَّبُّ تَعَالَى يَقُولُ: دَعُوا عَبْدِي، فَأَنَا أَعْلَمُ بِهِ، إِذْ أَنْشَأْتُهُ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ۔ إِنْ كَانَ عَبْدَكُمْ فَشَأْنُكُمْ بِهِ، وَإِنْ كَانَ عَبْدِي فَمِنِّي وَإِلَيَّ۔ وَعِزَّتِي وَجَلَالِي لَئِنْ أَتَانِي لَيْلًا قَبِلْتُهُ، وَإِنْ أَتَانِي نَهَارًا قَبِلْتُهُ، وَإِنْ تَقَرَّبَ مِنِّي شِبْرًا تَقَرَّبْتُ مِنْهُ ذِرَاعًا، وَإِنْ تَقَرَّبَ مِنِّي ذِرَاعًا تَقَرَّبْتُ مِنْهُ بَاعًا، وَإِنْ مَشَى إِلَيَّ هَرْوَلْتُ إِلَيْهِ. وَإِنِ اسْتَغْفَرَنِي غَفَرْتُ لَهُ، وَإِنْ تَابَ إِلَيَّ تُبْتُ عَلَيْهِ

atoms.text_en: My companions are like the stars; whichever of them you follow, you will be guided.
atoms.gloss: My companions are like the stars; whichever of them you follow, you will be guided.

atoms.text_en: My companions are like the stars—whichever of them you emulate, you will be guided.
atoms.gloss: My companions are like the stars—whichever of them you emulate, you will be guided.
